sessions.title: Move & Reset
sessions.details: Lunchtime movement class (60 min) with Yael Flexer. Open to adults of all ages and abilities; improvisation, guided exercises, and gentle movement. £12 per session; £40 for the 4-week course (booking fee applies on site).

sessions.title: DABKEROOTS
sessions.details: DabkeRoots class on 11 April 2026. Check the organiser page for the current time and venue details.

sessions.title: How the voice moves: a poetry & dance improv workshop
sessions.details: Poetry and dance improvisation workshop. Omnibus Theatre, SW4 0QW.

sessions.title: Bianca Scout and Elena Isolini (Daughter Mary) Dance Workshop
sessions.details: Workshop developing “Act 1” of Daughter Mary / Anon’s Ballet: classical ballet with unrestricted gestures, scene making and character study. 35 Britannia Row, London. Part of Mica Georgis’s residency at The Writers’ Room, Florence Trust. Open to dancers and non-dancers. N.B. venue not accessible (studio up two flights of stairs). Tickets on Gel.

sessions.title: Zahra Van Nguyen Dance Workshop
sessions.details: Somatics and improvisational workshop: movement as narrative, lyricism and dramatic structures, text and score. 35 Britannia Row, London. Part of Mica Georgis’s residency at The Writers’ Room, Florence Trust. Open to dancers and non-dancers. N.B. venue not accessible (studio up two flights of stairs). Tickets on Gel.

sessions.title: Mya Cavner x Ballet for Non-Dancers Dance Workshop
sessions.details: Ballet for Non-Dancers: intentional movement, sensuality and presence. 35 Britannia Row, London. Part of Mica Georgis’s residency at The Writers’ Room, Florence Trust. Open to dancers and non-dancers. N.B. venue not accessible (studio up two flights of stairs). Tickets on Gel.

sessions.title: Jules Cunningham Dance Workshop
sessions.details: Workshop with Jules Cunningham: form, gender identity and collaborative movement language. 35 Britannia Row, London. Part of Mica Georgis’s residency at The Writers’ Room, Florence Trust. Open to dancers and non-dancers.

sessions.title: UK Dancers for Palestine events
sessions.details: Campaign updates, ticketed events, and community actions from UK Dancers for Palestine. Check the organiser page or Instagram for the latest activity.

sessions.title: Professional Class
sessions.details: *Please note, professional class now runs from Tuesday to Thursday (8:00am-9:30am). Our daily classes for professional-level dancers are led each week by a different artist from the international dance community. Each teacher brings their own style and technique to class, from their experience performing with world-leading dance companies and choreographers. With a strong focus on honing and maintaining technique, you’ll be challenged and inspired amongst peers. Professional class is ‘drop in’ and be booked by the day. You can still drop in and pay at Rambert, however as our spaces are strictly limited we recommend you book online before travelling to the building. Check out our line up for Summer 2026 below: w/c 20th April – Jose Agudo w/c 27th April – Dylan Tedaldi w/c 4th May – Ching-Ying Chien w/c 11th May – David Blake w/c 18th May – Gerrard Martin w/c 25th May – Eileih Muir w/c 1st June – Livia Massarelli w/c 8th June – Finleigh Zack w/c 15th June – Sadé Alleyne w/c 22nd June – Kieran Brown w/c 29th June – Noah Oast w/c 6th July – Belinda Roy Terms and Conditions of Booking If you need to cancel your booking, you can do so up to 48 hours before a drop in for a full refund in class credits by logging into your Momence account. If you'd prefer a monetary refund, just email us to let us know.

sessions.title: Line Dancing Beginners
sessions.details: Hold ‘em like they do in Texas with our new line dance class. Learn a range of short, up tempo line dance routines to a range of music including Country & Western classics from the likes of Dolly Parton and Shania Twain, to current TikTok trends like Boots on the Ground and House of Bamboo. Heel tapping, thigh slapping and suitable for all experience and fitness levels, this class is all the Thursday evening fun you need to get ready for the weekend. Wear whatever is comfortable but to save our lovely dance floors, please wear jazz shoes or light soled trainers and leave your cowboy boots at the door. sure you have removed jewellery before class, and long hair is tied back. Please note, as the studios are used by our professional dancers we ask that you refrain from using hair products and body lotions as these can make the floor slippery. sessions.title: Ballet Improvers
sessions.details: Nina’s upbeat class will explore placement, spatial expansion, lyricism, and musicality. The focus will be on artistic expression, and how correct technique and coordination facilitates pace and speed. Nina began as an actress and dancer, appearing in over 60 TV, film, and stage productions before moving on to teach and coach ballet companies and dancers. Suitable if: you are 18+ and comfortable with the basics of Ballet and want to progress your technique with new challenges and combinations. We'd recommend around one year of ballet experience for this class. Building Information Classes take place at Rambert, 99 Upper Ground SE1 9PP. sessions.title: Ballroom Beginners
sessions.details: Izzi blends the old elegance of Hollywood with the notion of deconstructing gender stereotypes. Expect empowering movements that celebrate strength and confidence for everyone, in a supportive and inclusive space. Learn a selection of dances over the term – from Foxtrot to Tango and Samba to Jive. Get ready for a joyful cardio workout without even realising you’re exercising. Izzi is a professional Ballroom and Latin dance instructor. She focusses on making Ballroom and Latin dance fit for the 21st century by deconstructing the gender stereotypes and homophobia traditionally embedded within couple dancing and making the ballroom world fairer for everyone. Suitable for: all if you are 18+, including complete beginners. Building Information Classes take place at Rambert, 99 Upper Ground SE1 9PP. sessions.details: This class covers most of the classical mat Pilates exercises but with a more contemporary approach and variations to train. Symeon’s teaching approach encourages you to learn how to make personal choices for your body and progress or regress according to your needs. Props are used to either challenge or simplify certain exercises. Suitable for: all if you are 18+, including complete beginners. sessions.title: Contemporary Intermediate
sessions.details: In this class, Belinda will guide you through a series of exercises which blend the use of torso, fall and recovery, spatial pathways, flow and momentum. Drawing from her training in Cunningham, Limon and Release techniques, class will start with short technical exercises that build into more complex travelling steps, dynamic swings and longer choreographic phrases. In this class a sense of community and shared experience is created, providing an environment where participants feel safe, challenge themselves and above all, have fun. Suitable if: you are 18+ and you’ve taken a few months of contemporary dance classes before, are comfortable with the basics, and would like to develop your technique and creativity. Building Information Classes take place at Rambert, 99 Upper Ground SE1 9PP. We have a fully accessible building, including accessible toilets and showers. There is on-street parking for Blue Badge holders with step-free access to the building, and lifts to all levels. Changing facilities are available on the first floor. A water fountain is available on the second floor- please bring your own reuse-able bottle for this. You can wear whatever your are comfortable moving in, and you’re welcome to wear dance shoes if you have them (although no dark/black soles) or bare-feet. Please make sure you have removed jewellery before class, and long hair is tied back. Please note, as the studios are used by our professional dancers we ask that you refrain from using hair products and body lotions as these can make the floor slippery. Terms and Conditions of Booking If you need to cancel your booking, you can do so up to 48 hours before a drop in and up to 48 hours before the first class of a term booking for a full refund in class credits by logging into your Momence account. If you'd prefer a monetary refund, just email us to let us know.

sessions.title: Ballet Beginners
sessions.details: In this Beginners class, Nandita will share the basics and foundations of Ballet technique with a healthy and holistic approach encouraging supportive alignment, functionality and efficiency, focus, balance, coordination and strength. You will develop a deeper awareness of your body and relationship to the space, all while moving with musicality. As one of the few professional female ballet dancers of Indian heritage in the world, we’re so proud to welcome Nandita to our teaching faculty. After training at the Royal Ballet School, Nandita went on to perform with Zürcher Ballet, Victor Ullate Ballet, Ballet Black, Scottish Ballet and Compañia Nacional de Danza. Suitable if: you are 18+ and you’ve never taken a ballet class before, or been once or twice and want to refresh your memory. Building Information Classes take place at Rambert, 99 Upper Ground SE1 9PP. We have a fully accessible building, including accessible toilets and showers. Please make sure you have removed jewellery before class, and long hair is tied back. Please note, as the studios are used by our professional dancers we ask that you refrain from using hair products and body lotions as these can make the floor slippery. sessions.title: Line Dancing Beginners
sessions.details: Hold ‘em like they do in Texas with our new line dance class. Learn a range of short, up tempo line dance routines to a range of music including Country & Western classics from the likes of Dolly Parton and Shania Twain, to current TikTok trends like Boots on the Ground and House of Bamboo. Heel tapping, thigh slapping and suitable for all experience and fitness levels, this class is all the Thursday evening fun you need to get ready for the weekend. Wear whatever is comfortable but to save our lovely dance floors, please wear jazz shoes or light soled trainers and leave your cowboy boots at the door. sure you have removed jewellery before class, and long hair is tied back. sessions.title: Ballet Improvers
sessions.details: Nina’s upbeat class will explore placement, spatial expansion, lyricism, and musicality. The focus will be on artistic expression, and how correct technique and coordination facilitates pace and speed. Nina began as an actress and dancer, appearing in over 60 TV, film, and stage productions before moving on to teach and coach ballet companies and dancers. Suitable if: you are 18+ and comfortable with the basics of Ballet and want to progress your technique with new challenges and combinations. We'd recommend around one year of ballet experience for this class. Building Information Classes take place at Rambert, 99 Upper Ground SE1 9PP. sessions.title: Ballroom Beginners
sessions.details: Izzi blends the old elegance of Hollywood with the notion of deconstructing gender stereotypes. Expect empowering movements that celebrate strength and confidence for everyone, in a supportive and inclusive space. Learn a selection of dances over the term – from Foxtrot to Tango and Samba to Jive. Get ready for a joyful cardio workout without even realising you’re exercising. Izzi is a professional Ballroom and Latin dance instructor. She focusses on making Ballroom and Latin dance fit for the 21st century by deconstructing the gender stereotypes and homophobia traditionally embedded within couple dancing and making the ballroom world fairer for everyone. Suitable for: all if you are 18+, including complete beginners. Building Information Classes take place at Rambert, 99 Upper Ground SE1 9PP. sessions.title: Contemporary Intermediate
sessions.details: In this class, Belinda will guide you through a series of exercises which blend the use of torso, fall and recovery, spatial pathways, flow and momentum. Drawing from her training in Cunningham, Limon and Release techniques, class will start with short technical exercises that build into more complex travelling steps, dynamic swings and longer choreographic phrases. In this class a sense of community and shared experience is created, providing an environment where participants feel safe, challenge themselves and above all, have fun. Suitable if: you are 18+ and you’ve taken a few months of contemporary dance classes before, are comfortable with the basics, and would like to develop your technique and creativity. Building Information Classes take place at Rambert, 99 Upper Ground SE1 9PP. We have a fully accessible building, including accessible toilets and showers. sessions.title: Ballet Beginners
sessions.details: In this Beginners class, Nandita will share the basics and foundations of Ballet technique with a healthy and holistic approach encouraging supportive alignment, functionality and efficiency, focus, balance, coordination and strength. You will develop a deeper awareness of your body and relationship to the space, all while moving with musicality. As one of the few professional female ballet dancers of Indian heritage in the world, we’re so proud to welcome Nandita to our teaching faculty. After training at the Royal Ballet School, Nandita went on to perform with Zürcher Ballet, Victor Ullate Ballet, Ballet Black, Scottish Ballet and Compañia Nacional de Danza. Suitable if: you are 18+ and you’ve never taken a ballet class before, or been once or twice and want to refresh your memory. Building Information Classes take place at Rambert, 99 Upper Ground SE1 9PP. We have a fully accessible building, including accessible toilets and showers. sessions.title: Ballroom Beginners
sessions.details: Izzi blends the old elegance of Hollywood with the notion of deconstructing gender stereotypes. Expect empowering movements that celebrate strength and confidence for everyone, in a supportive and inclusive space. Learn a selection of dances over the term – from Foxtrot to Tango and Samba to Jive. Get ready for a joyful cardio workout without even realising you’re exercising. Izzi is a professional Ballroom and Latin dance instructor. She focusses on making Ballroom and Latin dance fit for the 21st century by deconstructing the gender stereotypes and homophobia traditionally embedded within couple dancing and making the ballroom world fairer for everyone. Suitable for: all if you are 18+, including complete beginners. Building Information Classes take place at Rambert, 99 Upper Ground SE1 9PP. We have a fully accessible building, including accessible toilets and showers. There is on-street parking for Blue Badge holders with step-free access to the building, and lifts to all levels. Changing facilities are available on the first floor. A water fountain is available on the second floor- please bring your own reuse-able bottle for this. You can wear whatever your are comfortable moving in, and you’re welcome to wear dance shoes if you have them (although no dark/black soles) or bare-feet. Please make sure you have removed jewellery before class, and long hair is tied back. Please note, as the studios are used by our professional dancers we ask that you refrain from using hair products and body lotions as these can make the floor slippery. sessions.title: Pilates Open Level
sessions.details: This class covers most of the classical mat Pilates exercises but with a more contemporary approach and variations to train. Symeon’s teaching approach encourages you to learn how to make personal choices for your body and progress or regress according to your needs. Props are used to either challenge or simplify certain exercises. Suitable for: all if you are 18+, including complete beginners. Building Information Classes take place at Rambert, 99 Upper Ground SE1 9PP. We have a fully accessible building, including accessible toilets and showers. sessions.title: Contemporary Intermediate
sessions.details: In this class, Belinda will guide you through a series of exercises which blend the use of torso, fall and recovery, spatial pathways, flow and momentum. Drawing from her training in Cunningham, Limon and Release techniques, class will start with short technical exercises that build into more complex travelling steps, dynamic swings and longer choreographic phrases. In this class a sense of community and shared experience is created, providing an environment where participants feel safe, challenge themselves and above all, have fun. Suitable if: you are 18+ and you’ve taken a few months of contemporary dance classes before, are comfortable with the basics, and would like to develop your technique and creativity. Building Information Classes take place at Rambert, 99 Upper Ground SE1 9PP. We have a fully accessible building, including accessible toilets and showers. There is on-street parking for Blue Badge holders with step-free access to the building, and lifts to all levels. Changing facilities are available on the first floor. A water fountain is available on the second floor- please bring your own reuse-able bottle for this. You can wear whatever your are comfortable moving in, and you’re welcome to wear dance shoes if you have them (although no dark/black soles) or bare-feet. Please make sure you have removed jewellery before class, and long hair is tied back. Please note, as the studios are used by our professional dancers we ask that you refrain from using hair products and body lotions as these can make the floor slippery. Terms and Conditions of Booking If you need to cancel your booking, you can do so up to 48 hours before a drop in and up to 48 hours before the first class of a term booking for a full refund in class credits by logging into your Momence account. If you'd prefer a monetary refund, just email us to let us know.

sessions.title: Ballet Beginners
sessions.details: In this Beginners class, Nandita will share the basics and foundations of Ballet technique with a healthy and holistic approach encouraging supportive alignment, functionality and efficiency, focus, balance, coordination and strength. You will develop a deeper awareness of your body and relationship to the space, all while moving with musicality. As one of the few professional female ballet dancers of Indian heritage in the world, we’re so proud to welcome Nandita to our teaching faculty. After training at the Royal Ballet School, Nandita went on to perform with Zürcher Ballet, Victor Ullate Ballet, Ballet Black, Scottish Ballet and Compañia Nacional de Danza. Suitable if: you are 18+ and you’ve never taken a ballet class before, or been once or twice and want to refresh your memory. Building Information Classes take place at Rambert, 99 Upper Ground SE1 9PP. We have a fully accessible building, including accessible toilets and showers. Please make sure you have removed jewellery before class, and long hair is tied back. Please note, as the studios are used by our professional dancers we ask that you refrain from using hair products and body lotions as these can make the floor slippery. sessions.title: Line Dancing Beginners
sessions.details: Hold ‘em like they do in Texas with our new line dance class. Learn a range of short, up tempo line dance routines to a range of music including Country & Western classics from the likes of Dolly Parton and Shania Twain, to current TikTok trends like Boots on the Ground and House of Bamboo. Heel tapping, thigh slapping and suitable for all experience and fitness levels, this class is all the Thursday evening fun you need to get ready for the weekend. Wear whatever is comfortable but to save our lovely dance floors, please wear jazz shoes or light soled trainers and leave your cowboy boots at the door. sure you have removed jewellery before class, and long hair is tied back. sessions.title: Ballroom Beginners
sessions.details: Izzi blends the old elegance of Hollywood with the notion of deconstructing gender stereotypes. Expect empowering movements that celebrate strength and confidence for everyone, in a supportive and inclusive space. Learn a selection of dances over the term – from Foxtrot to Tango and Samba to Jive. Get ready for a joyful cardio workout without even realising you’re exercising. Izzi is a professional Ballroom and Latin dance instructor. She focusses on making Ballroom and Latin dance fit for the 21st century by deconstructing the gender stereotypes and homophobia traditionally embedded within couple dancing and making the ballroom world fairer for everyone. Suitable for: all if you are 18+, including complete beginners. Building Information Classes take place at Rambert, 99 Upper Ground SE1 9PP. sessions.title: Contemporary Intermediate
sessions.details: In this class, Belinda will guide you through a series of exercises which blend the use of torso, fall and recovery, spatial pathways, flow and momentum. Drawing from her training in Cunningham, Limon and Release techniques, class will start with short technical exercises that build into more complex travelling steps, dynamic swings and longer choreographic phrases. In this class a sense of community and shared experience is created, providing an environment where participants feel safe, challenge themselves and above all, have fun. Suitable if: you are 18+ and you’ve taken a few months of contemporary dance classes before, are comfortable with the basics, and would like to develop your technique and creativity. sessions.title: Ballet Beginners
sessions.details: In this Beginners class, Nandita will share the basics and foundations of Ballet technique with a healthy and holistic approach encouraging supportive alignment, functionality and efficiency, focus, balance, coordination and strength. You will develop a deeper awareness of your body and relationship to the space, all while moving with musicality. As one of the few professional female ballet dancers of Indian heritage in the world, we’re so proud to welcome Nandita to our teaching faculty. After training at the Royal Ballet School, Nandita went on to perform with Zürcher Ballet, Victor Ullate Ballet, Ballet Black, Scottish Ballet and Compañia Nacional de Danza. sessions.title: Contemporary Intermediate
sessions.details: In this class, Belinda will guide you through a series of exercises which blend the use of torso, fall and recovery, spatial pathways, flow and momentum. Drawing from her training in Cunningham, Limon and Release techniques, class will start with short technical exercises that build into more complex travelling steps, dynamic swings and longer choreographic phrases. In this class a sense of community and shared experience is created, providing an environment where participants feel safe, challenge themselves and above all, have fun. Suitable if: you are 18+ and you’ve taken a few months of contemporary dance classes before, are comfortable with the basics, and would like to develop your technique and creativity. sessions.title: Ballet Beginners
sessions.details: In this Beginners class, Nandita will share the basics and foundations of Ballet technique with a healthy and holistic approach encouraging supportive alignment, functionality and efficiency, focus, balance, coordination and strength. You will develop a deeper awareness of your body and relationship to the space, all while moving with musicality. As one of the few professional female ballet dancers of Indian heritage in the world, we’re so proud to welcome Nandita to our teaching faculty. After training at the Royal Ballet School, Nandita went on to perform with Zürcher Ballet, Victor Ullate Ballet, Ballet Black, Scottish Ballet and Compañia Nacional de Danza. Suitable if: you are 18+ and you’ve never taken a ballet class before, or been once or twice and want to refresh your memory. sessions.details: Hold ‘em like they do in Texas with our new line dance class. Learn a range of short, up tempo line dance routines to a range of music including Country & Western classics from the likes of Dolly Parton and Shania Twain, to current TikTok trends like Boots on the Ground and House of Bamboo. Heel tapping, thigh slapping and suitable for all experience and fitness levels, this class is all the Thursday evening fun you need to get ready for the weekend. sessions.title: Ballroom Beginners
sessions.details: Izzi blends the old elegance of Hollywood with the notion of deconstructing gender stereotypes. Expect empowering movements that celebrate strength and confidence for everyone, in a supportive and inclusive space. Learn a selection of dances over the term – from Foxtrot to Tango and Samba to Jive. Get ready for a joyful cardio workout without even realising you’re exercising. Izzi is a professional Ballroom and Latin dance instructor. She focusses on making Ballroom and Latin dance fit for the 21st century by deconstructing the gender stereotypes and homophobia traditionally embedded within couple dancing and making the ballroom world fairer for everyone. sessions.title: Contemporary Intermediate
sessions.details: In this class, Belinda will guide you through a series of exercises which blend the use of torso, fall and recovery, spatial pathways, flow and momentum. Drawing from her training in Cunningham, Limon and Release techniques, class will start with short technical exercises that build into more complex travelling steps, dynamic swings and longer choreographic phrases. In this class a sense of community and shared experience is created, providing an environment where participants feel safe, challenge themselves and above all, have fun. Suitable if: you are 18+ and you’ve taken a few months of contemporary dance classes before, are comfortable with the basics, and would like to develop your technique and creativity. sessions.title: Ballet Beginners
sessions.details: In this Beginners class, Nandita will share the basics and foundations of Ballet technique with a healthy and holistic approach encouraging supportive alignment, functionality and efficiency, focus, balance, coordination and strength. You will develop a deeper awareness of your body and relationship to the space, all while moving with musicality. As one of the few professional female ballet dancers of Indian heritage in the world, we’re so proud to welcome Nandita to our teaching faculty. After training at the Royal Ballet School, Nandita went on to perform with Zürcher Ballet, Victor Ullate Ballet, Ballet Black, Scottish Ballet and Compañia Nacional de Danza. sessions.title: Contemporary Intermediate
sessions.details: In this class, Belinda will guide you through a series of exercises which blend the use of torso, fall and recovery, spatial pathways, flow and momentum. Drawing from her training in Cunningham, Limon and Release techniques, class will start with short technical exercises that build into more complex travelling steps, dynamic swings and longer choreographic phrases. In this class a sense of community and shared experience is created, providing an environment where participants feel safe, challenge themselves and above all, have fun. Suitable if: you are 18+ and you’ve taken a few months of contemporary dance classes before, are comfortable with the basics, and would like to develop your technique and creativity. sessions.title: Ballet Beginners
sessions.details: In this Beginners class, Nandita will share the basics and foundations of Ballet technique with a healthy and holistic approach encouraging supportive alignment, functionality and efficiency, focus, balance, coordination and strength. You will develop a deeper awareness of your body and relationship to the space, all while moving with musicality. As one of the few professional female ballet dancers of Indian heritage in the world, we’re so proud to welcome Nandita to our teaching faculty. After training at the Royal Ballet School, Nandita went on to perform with Zürcher Ballet, Victor Ullate Ballet, Ballet Black, Scottish Ballet and Compañia Nacional de Danza. Suitable if: you are 18+ and you’ve never taken a ballet class before, or been once or twice and want to refresh your memory. Expect empowering movements that celebrate strength and confidence for everyone, in a supportive and inclusive space. Learn a selection of dances over the term – from Foxtrot to Tango and Samba to Jive. Get ready for a joyful cardio workout without even realising you’re exercising. Izzi is a professional Ballroom and Latin dance instructor. sessions.details: Nina’s upbeat class will explore placement, spatial expansion, lyricism, and musicality. The focus will be on artistic expression, and how correct technique and coordination facilitates pace and speed. Nina began as an actress and dancer, appearing in over 60 TV, film, and stage productions before moving on to teach and coach ballet companies and dancers. Suitable if: you are 18+ and comfortable with the basics of Ballet and want to progress your technique with new challenges and combinations. We'd recommend around one year of ballet experience for this class. Building Information Classes take place at Rambert, 99 Upper Ground SE1 9PP. We have a fully accessible building, including accessible toilets and showers. There is on-street parking for Blue Badge holders with step-free access to the building, and lifts to all levels. Changing facilities are available on the first floor. A water fountain is available on the second floor- please bring your own reuse-able bottle for this. You can wear whatever your are comfortable moving in, and you’re welcome to wear dance shoes if you have them (although no dark/black soles) or bare-feet. Please make sure you have removed jewellery before class, and long hair is tied back. Please note, as the studios are used by our professional dancers we ask that you refrain from using hair products and body lotions as these can make the floor slippery. Terms and Conditions of Booking If you need to cancel your booking, you can do so up to 48 hours before a drop in and up to 48 hours before the first class of a term booking for a full refund in class credits by logging into your Momence account. If you'd prefer a monetary refund, just email us to let us know.

sessions.title: ProDance class with Finleigh Zack
sessions.details: Finleigh’s teaching practice approaches movement through a mindful and emotionally connected lens. Her classes begin with guided improvisation and somatic exploration, allowing dancers to tune into their physical and emotional bodies before building toward more energized contemporary phrase work. By starting from sensations, dancers are invited to discover authentic movement impulses that later expand into expansive choreography. Throughout the class, mindfulness tools such as breath and reflection are used to deepen awareness and support dancers in connecting to the emotional body of movement. These practices create space for dancers to explore themes of self, relationships, and community while remaining grounded in their own experience. Improvisational tasks gradually develop into structured phrase work, allowing dancers to shift from introspective exploration into full-bodied contemporary movement. Finleigh’s classes are designed to be both reflective and energizing, offering artists a process that nurtures creativity while challenging physicality. By the end of the class, dancers will experience how Finleigh Zack Dance develops material, from investigation to expressive contemporary movement, while cultivating a movement practice rooted in mindfulness, emotional awareness, and connection.

sessions.title: YIN  WITH MYOFASCIAL RELEASE
sessions.details: Yin Myofascial Release (Yin MFR) is a slow, nourishing practice that combines long-held Yin poses with massage balls to gently melt away deep tension in the body. We’ll settle into passive stretches for a few minutes at a time, using props to apply steady, mindful pressure to areas like the hips, back, shoulders and feet. This helps release tight fascia, improve mobility, ease soreness and leave you feeling more spacious and hydrated from the inside out. It’s not about pushing, it’s about softening. As the body unwinds, the nervous system shifts into “rest and digest” mode, helping you feel grounded, calm and deeply relaxed. Suitable for most levels, this class is a beautiful way to care for your body, move more freely, and give yourself the reset you didn’t know you needed.

sessions.title: Improvisation into Choreography Workshop
sessions.details: This workshop is designed for those who wish to deepen and challenge their dance practice within a playful and reflective environment. Drawing from over a decade of developing her improvisational and choreographic practice, Faye invites participants to explore movement through curiosity, offering puzzles and challenges for both body and mind that cultivate complexity, receptiveness, control, spontaneity and anatomical awareness. Following a gentle mental and physical warm-up, Faye guides the group through a series of improvisation tasks, with moments for reflection and processing throughout the workshop through dialogue, drawing and writing. She will also share choreographed material from her works, creating an opportunity to explore how improvisation can shape and enrich the experience of dancing set material. photo credit: Kirsten McTernan.

sessions.title: Flying Low
sessions.details: Developed by David Zambrano, Flying Low is a floorwork technique that teaches smooth transitions into and out of the floor using spirals, sliding, and fluid sequences. It builds the foundation for effortless connection between the body and its surroundings—integral to the art of Passing Through.

sessions.title: ProDance Class with Attila Andrasi
sessions.details: Elegant rhythm The class explores the concept of virtuosity and the sensation of fluidity, drawing inspiration from Hungarian and Balkan traditional folk dance movement patterns and rhythms. The main objective is to help participants achieve a hyper-performative momentum and gain experience from the ecstatic and recessive state. It also encourages experimenting with a dignified, elegant posture while in motion, within the context of decorative abstraction. Participants will learn movement combinations through improvisation scores and short phrases.

sessions.title: YANG TO YIN
sessions.details: This class will flow through the energies of Yang into Yin. The first half will be focused on dynamic vinyasa-style asana to connect with and work the physical body through strong postures, an awareness of breath, a deep connection to sensation and body-mindfulness. The second half is designed to stimulate the parasympathetic nervous system with long floor-based postures, supported by meditation and mindfulness techniques to calm, ground and reset. ***Please note: Online booking window closes 30 minutes before class starts.

sessions.title: ProDance Class with Attila Andrasi
sessions.details: Elegant rhythm The class explores the concept of virtuosity and the sensation of fluidity, drawing inspiration from Hungarian and Balkan traditional folk dance movement patterns and rhythms. The main objective is to help participants achieve a hyper-performative momentum and gain experience from the ecstatic and recessive state. It also encourages experimenting with a dignified, elegant posture while in motion, within the context of decorative abstraction. Participants will learn movement combinations through improvisation scores and short phrases.

sessions.title: ROCKET
sessions.details: Rocket is deeply rooted in Ashtanga. It is a series of standing, balancing and inverted postures all linked by vinyasas (movement flows), incorporating basic poses with challenging arm balances, inversions and back bends Inversions are lot of fun, but they also prove to be some of the most challenging postures. Some of the upside down postures are designed to energise us, others are designed to calm us down. No matter if you are a newbie or an old hat to inversions this class will be fun, will get you sweating and see the world from another perspective -upside down.

sessions.title: ProDance Class with Attila Andrasi
sessions.details: Elegant rhythm The class explores the concept of virtuosity and the sensation of fluidity, drawing inspiration from Hungarian and Balkan traditional folk dance movement patterns and rhythms. The main objective is to help participants achieve a hyper-performative momentum and gain experience from the ecstatic and recessive state. It also encourages experimenting with a dignified, elegant posture while in motion, within the context of decorative abstraction. Participants will learn movement combinations through improvisation scores and short phrases.

sessions.title: LATIN-FUSION DANCE
sessions.details: A joyful, high-energy class blending Latin dance styles with contemporary movement and expressive flow. In this 60-minute session, you’ll explore rhythm, musicality, and full-body movement through accessible choreography and playful combinations. Open to all levels, the class offers options and variations so you can move at your own pace—whether you’re new to dance or looking to deepen your groove. Expect a supportive atmosphere, a good sweat, and plenty of space to connect with the music, your body, and the joy of movement. No partner needed. Come as you are, leave energised and inspired.

sessions.title: Gaga People
sessions.details: Gaga is the movement language developed by Ohad Naharin throughout many years, parallel to his work as a choreographer and artistic director (1990-2018) of Batsheva Dance Company. Gaga originated from Naharin’s need to communicate with his dancers and his curiosity in the ongoing research of movement. Gaga classes are predicated on a deep listening to the body and to physical sensations. The instructions are deployed to increase awareness of and further amplify sensation, and rather than turning from one prompt to another, information is layered, building into a multisensory, physically challenging experience. While many instructions are imbued with rich imagery, the research of Gaga is fundamentally physical, insisting on a specific process of embodiment. Inside this shared research, the improvisational nature of the exploration enables each participant’s deeply personal connection with Gaga. Throughout the class, participants are guided by a series of evocative instructions deployed to increase awareness of and further amplify sensation. Rather than turning from one prompt to another, information is layered, building into a multisensory, physically challenging experience. While many instructions are imbued with rich imagery, the research of Gaga is fundamentally physical, insisting on a specific process of embodiment. Inside this shared research, the improvisational nature of the exploration enables each participant’s deeply personal connection with Gaga. Participants should wear comfortable clothes and be prepared to dance barefoot or in socks. It is advisable to bring a bottle of water and a towel for use after class.

sessions.title: ProDance Class with Ozzy & Tiegan
sessions.details: Sweat, Strength and Totality - the journey of class. Through diving into sensation and groove, we will explore story telling as we find humour, character and individuality through improvisational research. Class with explore a range of textures and images as we move through guided improvisation, engaging the core challenging the body. Through crossings and set phrases we will explore the totality of movement, from the smallest gesture to the largest groove, as we move through character and humour driven phrases. This is a space for fun and accessible play, to research and laugh together.

sessions.title: ProDance Class with Orla Hardie
sessions.details: Orla’s teaching practice is an exploration of the imagination, using texture and self-caricature as pathways to connect to movement. Supported by an array of music genres and guided improvisation, we will move through a range of dynamics from gooey totality to squeaky articulation. Moving through set material, we’ll dissolve in and out of the floor, getting down, sweaty, and fully immersed. Together we create a space where we can dive into the mind, liberate the body, and play.

sessions.title: PARTNERING WORKSHOP
sessions.details: This contemporary partnering workshop explores trust, communication, and physical connection through movement. Beginning with a guided warm-up focused on mobility, strength, and stability, participants will prepare their bodies with floorwork, weight transfer, spinal articulation, and gentle inversions. We then move into partner-based exercises that build trust and awareness through weight-sharing and safe, supportive grips. As confidence develops, participants will explore the mechanics of lifting and being lifted, breaking down technique to ensure accessibility, safety, and clarity for all bodies. The session culminates in learning a collaborative movement phrase, integrating the skills developed throughout the workshop while encouraging communication and adaptability between partners. This class creates a supportive environment where participants can expand their movement vocabulary, deepen connection, and discover new possibilities within partnering work.

sessions.title: ProDance class with Marla King
sessions.details: The class will weave through guided improvisation and structured movement sequences, with the aim of awakening our sequentiality, a sense of flow in the body, and expanding the qualities, sensations and possibilities that feel open to us in our dancing. A space to be playful, engage our imagination, welcome challenge, follow what lights us up, and nurture our connection with one another. Exploring and experiencing the strength and power that is held in softness and sensitivity. Expect a sweaty, spirally, wavy, connective morning with ecology-inspired movement explorations and some wholesome music!

sessions.title: Countertechnique class &  Physical Theatre WORKSHOP
sessions.details: This dynamic workshop with Borna Babić begins with a Countertechnique class, focusing on efficiency, clarity, and expanding movement possibilities through the organisation of the body in space. Participants will work with tools that enhance coordination, awareness, and availability for movement, building both physical stamina and adaptability. The second half of the workshop shifts into physical theatre exploration, investigating the connection between intention, emotion, and movement. Through guided improvisation tasks and playful games, participants will be encouraged to deepen their individuality and discover how their personal impulses can inform performance. We will explore two equally valuable approaches within the creative process: allowing physicality to evoke intention and emotion, and embodying thought or emotional states to generate movement. Particular attention will be given to researching different physical qualities and textures, and how these can be integrated into partnering work. In an open, supportive, and curious environment, we will challenge habitual movement patterns and cultivate sensitivity to detail, authenticity, and performative presence.

sessions.title: ProDance Class with Billy Maxwell-Taylor
sessions.details: Through improvisation, crossings, and a focus on spaciousness, this class invites the interplay of tranquility and vitality - the humming energy of the body. How can we move without judgement and with deep listening? Underscored by ambient music and a touch of bubbly jazz, this space offers an opportunity to ground into our dance practice while developing our physical foundations through mobilization, soft core stabilization and meditation. Expect invitations like "take time", "notice" and "ride the joy of this disappearing moment".

sessions.title: ProDance Class with Billy Maxwell-Taylor
sessions.details: Through improvisation, crossings, and a focus on spaciousness, this class invites the interplay of tranquility and vitality - the humming energy of the body. How can we move without judgement and with deep listening? Underscored by ambient music and a touch of bubbly jazz, this space offers an opportunity to ground into our dance practice while developing our physical foundations through mobilization, soft core stabilization and meditation. Expect invitations like "take time", "notice" and "ride the joy of this disappearing moment".

sessions.title: LATIN-FUSION DANCE
sessions.details: A joyful, high-energy class blending Latin dance styles with contemporary movement and expressive flow. In this 60-minute session, you’ll explore rhythm, musicality, and full-body movement through accessible choreography and playful combinations. Open to all levels, the class offers options and variations so you can move at your own pace—whether you’re new to dance or looking to deepen your groove. Expect a supportive atmosphere, a good sweat, and plenty of space to connect with the music, your body, and the joy of movement. No partner needed. Come as you are, leave energised and inspired.

sessions.title: GAGA.PEOPLE
sessions.details: Gaga/dancers deepens dancers’ awareness of physical sensations, expands their palette of available movement options, enhances their ability to modulate their energy and engage their explosive power, and enriches their movement quality with a wide range of textures. The classes are built on the same principles as Gaga/people classes but also employ the specific vocabulary and skills that are part and parcel of a dancer’s knowledge. The layering of familiar skills with Gaga tasks presents dancers with fresh challenges, and throughout the class, teachers prompt the dancers to visit more unfamiliar places and ways of moving as well, unlocking the endlessness of possibilities. Dancers are guided to connect their effort to pleasure and to discover the virtue of silliness.

sessions.title: ProDance Class with Richard Chappell
sessions.details: Richard's class starts with mobilising, expanding and stretching the body through repetitive movement patterns and improvisational tasks, connecting to breath. After this, exercises are looped across the studio, connecting dancers to the ground and encouraging continuous passing of weight between the hands and feet. Spirals are used to build up momentum, coordination and precision and small phrases connect material together. The class concludes a full bodied and playful final phrase. Richard's class is designed to be energetic and challenging, whilst also being accessible and adaptable for different energy levels and needs.

sessions.title: Unlocking Movement: From Simplicity to Complexity
sessions.details: This workshop centres on improvisation practices developed over several years, offering clear and accessible tasks that invite dancers to tap into their creativity and imagination. Through guided exploration, participants will open up new possibilities and discover fresh ways of moving. Dancers will move between refined, minimal movement and more layered, intricate physicality, expanding both range and awareness. The session encourages each participant to develop their own movement language, exploring it with sincerity, curiosity, and pleasure in a supportive environment.

sessions.title: ProDance class with Rose Sall Sao
sessions.details: Release / Unleash In this physically challenging contemporary class there will be a strong focus on dynamics, explosiveness and musicality. Through improvisation we will encourage playfulness, imagination and movement potential. The class includes exercises that allow grounding and connecting with our core to find ease and silence before speed and explosion. Rose will offer tools to move with agility, efficiency and fluidity whilst breaking habits and increasing awareness.

This helps release tight fascia, improve mobility, ease soreness and leave you feeling more spacious and hydrated from the inside out. It’s not about pushing, it’s about softening. As the body unwinds, the nervous system shifts into “rest and digest” mode, helping you feel grounded, calm and deeply relaxed. Suitable for most levels, this class is a beautiful way to care for your body, move more freely, and give yourself the reset you didn’t know you needed.

sessions.title: ROCKET
sessions.details: Rocket is deeply rooted in Ashtanga. It is a series of standing, balancing and inverted postures all linked by vinyasas (movement flows), incorporating basic poses with challenging arm balances, inversions and back bends Inversions are lot of fun, but they also prove to be some of the most challenging postures. Some of the upside down postures are designed to energise us, others are designed to calm us down. No matter if you are a newbie or an old hat to inversions this class will be fun, will get you sweating and see the world from another perspective -upside down.

sessions.title: LATIN-FUSION DANCE
sessions.details: A joyful, high-energy class blending Latin dance styles with contemporary movement and expressive flow. In this 60-minute session, you’ll explore rhythm, musicality, and full-body movement through accessible choreography and playful combinations. Open to all levels, the class offers options and variations so you can move at your own pace—whether you’re new to dance or looking to deepen your groove. Expect a supportive atmosphere, a good sweat, and plenty of space to connect with the music, your body, and the joy of movement. No partner needed. Come as you are, leave energised and inspired.

Open to all levels, the class offers options and variations so you can move at your own pace—whether you’re new to dance or looking to deepen your groove. Expect a supportive atmosphere, a good sweat, and plenty of space to connect with the music, your body, and the joy of movement. No partner needed. Come as you are, leave energised and inspired.

sessions.title: YIN  WITH MYOFASCIAL RELEASE
sessions.details: Yin Myofascial Release (Yin MFR) is a slow, nourishing practice that combines long-held Yin poses with massage balls to gently melt away deep tension in the body. We’ll settle into passive stretches for a few minutes at a time, using props to apply steady, mindful pressure to areas like the hips, back, shoulders and feet. This helps release tight fascia, improve mobility, ease soreness and leave you feeling more spacious and hydrated from the inside out. It’s not about pushing, it’s about softening. As the body unwinds, the nervous system shifts into “rest and digest” mode, helping you feel grounded, calm and deeply relaxed. Suitable for most levels, this class is a beautiful way to care for your body, move more freely, and give yourself the reset you didn’t know you needed.

sessions.title: ROCKET
sessions.details: Rocket is deeply rooted in Ashtanga. It is a series of standing, balancing and inverted postures all linked by vinyasas (movement flows), incorporating basic poses with challenging arm balances, inversions and back bends Inversions are lot of fun, but they also prove to be some of the most challenging postures. Some of the upside down postures are designed to energise us, others are designed to calm us down. No matter if you are a newbie or an old hat to inversions this class will be fun, will get you sweating and see the world from another perspective -upside down.

sessions.title: LindyHopEastLdn with Angela Andrew
sessions.details: 12 January 2026 - 06 July 2026

sessions.title: Friday Core Practice Group - Spring Term 1
sessions.details: Elizabeth House, 2 Hurlock Street, London, N5 1ED • Each week will cover a combination of techniques for falling, rolling, lifting and communication with a partner. The end of every class will leave plenty of time for free Contact dancing. Intermediate/advanced students with one year of CI experience. https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-senhf-20260123110000

sessions.title: Monday Beginners Level 1 (Course of 6 classes)
sessions.details: Acland Burghley School Sports Centre, Churchill Road, London, NW5 1AN • Mondays 05/01, 12/01, 19/01, 26/01, 02/02 and 09/02 from 6:30 to 8:30pm. Classes taught by Rick Nodine. The course is a carefully crafted syllabus that will support beginners in understanding the conceptual and physical world of Contact Improvisation (CI) so that they can trust their own opinions about what they want as they dive into the CI experience. Connecting with other people is at the core of this practice and you will learn how CI roots connection in the reality of touch and movement. Class 1) A talk about what CI is and what to expect from it, a lead somatic warm-up and a solo physical exploration about moving comfortably in close connection with the floor, exercises about communicating with a partner through touch and physical listening, discussion of consensual touch and freedom of choice in dancing, a space for questions and concerns to be expressed and addressed. Class 2) We will learn some simple patterns that open different body surfaces to meeting in contact. Exercises that clarify how to get in and out of dances will strengthen your agency in following your interests within a group. Class 3) Exercises about exchanging body weight with a partner will be the beginning of the thrill of taking risks, understanding that good communication leads to pleasure in weight exchange and a lack of communication leads to discomfort, feeling your strength and understanding your limits. Class 4) Exercises about how to fall safely, learning more CI vocabulary to expand possibilities in physical communication, building confidence in starting and ending dances. Class 5) We will explore creativity and improvisation so that we can maintain joy and curiosity in our dancing 6) Understanding Jamming and how to decode its rules and etiquette, responses to specific questions and a discussion about transitioning to open classes and jams. Integrating previous learning into free dancing and connecting to fun and risk in movement. https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-sqhh-20260126183000

sessions.title: Monday Beginners Level 1 (single class)
sessions.details: Acland Burghley School Sports Centre, Churchill Road, London, NW5 1AN • Mondays, 6:30 to 8:30. These classes aim to support new dancers in learning the basics so they can feel confident about joining the Open Wednesday class and Jam. I will make it clear how to communicate with a partner through touch. I will support beginners in understanding the conceptual and physical world of CI so that they can trust their own opinions about what they want as they dive into the CI experience. Class 4) Exercises about how to fall safely, learning more CI vocabulary to expand possibilities in physical communication, building confidence in starting and ending dances. https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-s0ks-20260126183000

sessions.title: Monday Beginners Level 1+2 (Course of 12 classes)
sessions.details: Acland Burghley School Sports Centre, Churchill Road, London, NW5 1AN • Mondays 05/01, 12/01, 19/01, 26/01, 02/02 and 09/02 (Beginners 1) And Mondays 23/02, 02/03, 09/03, 16/03, 23/03 and 30/03 (Beginners 2) from 6:30 to 8:30pm. The course is a carefully crafted syllabus that will support beginners in understanding the conceptual and physical world of Contact Improvisation (CI) so that they can trust their own opinions about what they want as they dive into the CI experience. Connecting with other people is at the core of this practice and you will learn how CI roots connection in the reality of touch and movement. Class 1) A talk about what CI is and what to expect from it, a lead somatic warm-up and a solo physical exploration about moving comfortably in close connection with the floor, exercises about communicating with a partner through touch and physical listening, discussion of consensual touch and freedom of choice in dancing, a space for questions and concerns to be expressed and addressed. Class 2) We will learn some simple patterns that open different body surfaces to meeting in contact. Exercises that clarify how to get in and out of dances will strengthen your agency in following your interests within a group. Class 3) Exercises about exchanging body weight with a partner will be the beginning of the thrill of taking risks, understanding that good communication leads to pleasure in weight exchange and a lack of communication leads to discomfort, feeling your strength and understanding your limits. Class 4) Exercises about how to fall safely, learning more CI vocabulary to expand possibilities in physical communication, building confidence in starting and ending dances. Class 5) We will explore creativity and improvisation so that we can maintain joy and curiosity in our dancing 6) Understanding Jamming and how to decode its rules and etiquette, responses to specific questions and a discussion about transitioning to open classes and jams. Integrating previous learning into free dancing and connecting to fun and risk in movement. https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-sqhh-20260202183000

sessions.title: Saturday CI Workshop: Jam Skills
sessions.details: Acland Burghley School Sports Centre, Churchill Road, London, NW5 1AN • This workshop is about how to get more out of Jamming. CI Jams are exciting spaces open to possibilities, but how do we access those possibilities? We will work with strategies for getting in and out of dances, enjoying being lost and look at what it means to be available for the unexpected to happen. We will unpack some jamming etiquette and how to avoid being a ‘jerk’ in a jam. There will be structured improvisation exercises that will explore jam scenarios and plenty of jamming with prompts to help you expand your jamming persona. As always with my workshops, you can choose your own level of challenge. Technical study will evolve into free Contact Dancing, where we can explore and integrate new learnings into improvisation. https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-sqtf-20260207143000

sessions.title: Contact Improvisation Intensive With Rick Nodine: Training Ground, Winter Edition
sessions.details: Acland Burghley School Sports Centre, Churchill Road, London, NW5 1AN • London, Thursday February 19th to Sunday February 22nd, 10h-17h In this intensive 4 days of study we will explore the ground that CI dances on. We will build our connect to earth, through the floor, with solo Somatics and simple but essential patterns that underpin the CI body. We will grow confidence in falling, rolling and inverting ourselves so that our partner dances can expand beyond habitual limits. Extended contact dancing guided by scores that demand connection through principles will guide us to the edges we need to work at. We will also reclaim the floor as playground for fresh explorations of the form and the pleasures of improvisation for its own sake. Time to explore, space to discover and days to build new skills will lead to questions and some answers about the dances you are looking for. https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-sbe5w-20260219100000

sessions.title: Bound & Unbound Weekend, Spring Edition: Exploring the Edges of Contact Improvisation
sessions.details: Anatomie Studio, Arch 113 Unit 17 Station Passage, London, SE15 2JR • A three day intensive with Jamus Wood & Rick Nodine. Contact Improvisation, Shibari & BMC®. In this short Intensive we will take time to explore some of the territory at the edges of Contact Improvisation. Exploring aspects of the dance that often go unrecognised or don’t receive quality attention. Contact improvisation, led by Rick, will be at the heart of our practice and the ground of our shared language. We will spend the first part of our time together dropping into inner and outer awareness through Body Mind Centering® exercises and perspectives. The intention is to bring a felt sense of embodiment and a deep listening to our dance. Later we will draw inspiration from Shibari (Japanese Rope Play) with Jamus. Initially, we’ll focus on the sensations of our bodies in relation to one another, without ropes yet through physical limitations, exploring binding and unbinding, being held and being free. We will then introduce ropes, engaging in Ichinawa (one rope technique) to explore further the qualities of resistance and surrender, stillness and breath, gravity and flow. A key aspect of our workshop will be The Switch —a playful exploration of roles as we transition between being bound and binding with one’s body. We will slow down and zoom in, lingering in each role for some time. Also taking moments when neither of us are in a role. We will emphasise consent throughout our time together. Consent will be taught as a practice so that we can dance within our limits while finding and exploring our edges together. We’ll conclude each day with an exploratory jam, allowing space to integrate what we've explored and to express ourselves freely, weaving our individual journeys into a collective tapestry of creativity and joy. Join us in this supportive circle of explorers. Together, let’s celebrate the borders of Contact Improvisation with Shibari, deepening our connections through movement, trust and shared discovery. Limited tickets available https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-stnbu-20260327183000

The course is a carefully crafted syllabus that will support beginners in understanding the conceptual and physical world of Contact Improvisation (CI) so that they can trust their own opinions about what they want as they dive into the CI experience. Connecting with other people is at the core of this practice and you will learn how CI roots connection in the reality of touch and movement. Class 1) A talk about what CI is and what to expect from it, a lead somatic warm-up and a solo physical exploration about moving comfortably in close connection with the floor, exercises about communicating with a partner through touch and physical listening, discussion of consensual touch and freedom of choice in dancing, a space for questions and concerns to be expressed and addressed. Class 2) We will learn some simple patterns that open different body surfaces to meeting in contact. Exercises that clarify how to get in and out of dances will strengthen your agency in following your interests within a group. Class 3) Exercises about exchanging body weight with a partner will be the beginning of the thrill of taking risks, understanding that good communication leads to pleasure in weight exchange and a lack of communication leads to discomfort, feeling your strength and understanding your limits. Class 4) Exercises about how to fall safely, learning more CI vocabulary to expand possibilities in physical communication, building confidence in starting and ending dances. Class 5) Understanding Jamming and how to decode its rules and etiquette, responses to specific questions and a discussion about transitioning to open classes and jams. Integrating previous learning into free dancing and connecting to fun and risk in movement. https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-sis0w-20260504183000

sessions.title: Saturday CI Workshop: Performing Oneself
sessions.details: Acland Burghley School Sports Centre, Churchill Road, London, NW5 1AN • This workshop is about being an audience and having an audience. Showing up and witnessing. Seeing and being seen, being yourself and being in a group. The focus will not be on looking good but rather on the way in which being seen supports us in seeing ourselves. We will frame witnessing each other dance as an expanded way of connecting. We will investigate relating to multiple partners at the same time and getting in and out of contact with a partner while maintaining the integrity of our own dance. We will move on to ensemble scores for improvising together. We will look at turning our own desires into action and how these individual expressions of creativity can coexist in a shared space with others. Issues or blocks related to fear, failure, taking space and confusion will be welcomed and fuel the training of our agency and awareness. As always with my workshops, you can choose your own level of challenge. Technical study will evolve into free Contact Dancing, where we can explore and integrate new learnings into improvisation. https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-sn3d0-20260516143000

sessions.title: Summer CI retreat with Rick Nodine, Tender Agility, Gaia Terra
sessions.details: Gaia Terra, Via Francesco Petrarca, 45, Rivignano Teor, UD, 33061 • It is called Tender Agility, and it's all in the name: tender: /ˈtɛndə/ 1. showing kindness and affection agility: /əˈdʒɪlɪti/ 1. ability to move quickly and easily It will be a 6 day CI intensive (24-30 August 2026) in the North of Italy, with workshops, bodywork and somatics sessions, Jams, hang out in the sun and swim in the river. We have been wanting to collaborate with Gaia Terra for a long time, so we are excited about spending a week in this Ecovillage nested in beautiful countryside, equipped with a state-of-the-art dance studio and gardens where they grow the food we will eat. CI Workshops will include: Somatic exploration CI technique/patterns and skills Exploration space Where? Gaia Terra, in the North of Italy. Gaia Terra is an Ecovillage nested in beautiful nature equipped with a state-of-the-art dance studio, beautiful surroundings and a river! Gaia Terra is more than just a site that is hosting us. It is an experience that aligns with the values of our community. With the values we aim to support about sharing, caring, growth and respect for nature. All participants will be asked to become a member of Gaia Terra (EUR 5 membership fee to pay upon arrival) and to contribute to the life of the Ecovillage with two 30 minutes slots of volunteering during their stay. https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-smmkt-20260824000000

sessions.title: Bound & Unbound Weekend, Autumn Edition: Exploring the Edges of Contact Improvisation
sessions.details: Anatomie Studio, Arch 113 Unit 17 Station Passage, London, SE15 2JR • A three day intensive with Jamus Wood & Rick Nodine. Contact Improvisation, Shibari & BMC®. In this short Intensive we will take time to explore some of the territory at the edges of Contact Improvisation. Exploring aspects of the dance that often go unrecognised or don’t receive quality attention. Contact improvisation, led by Rick, will be at the heart of our practice and the ground of our shared language. We will spend the first part of our time together dropping into inner and outer awareness through Body Mind Centering® exercises and perspectives. The intention is to bring a felt sense of embodiment and a deep listening to our dance. Later we will draw inspiration from Shibari (Japanese Rope Play) with Jamus. Initially, we’ll focus on the sensations of our bodies in relation to one another, without ropes yet through physical limitations, exploring binding and unbinding, being held and being free. We will then introduce ropes, engaging in Ichinawa (one rope technique) to explore further the qualities of resistance and surrender, stillness and breath, gravity and flow. A key aspect of our workshop will be The Switch —a playful exploration of roles as we transition between being bound and binding with one’s body. We will slow down and zoom in, lingering in each role for some time. Also taking moments when neither of us are in a role. We will emphasise consent throughout our time together. Consent will be taught as a practice so that we can dance within our limits while finding and exploring our edges together. We’ll conclude each day with an exploratory jam, allowing space to integrate what we've explored and to express ourselves freely, weaving our individual journeys into a collective tapestry of creativity and joy. Join us in this supportive circle of explorers. Together, let’s celebrate the borders of Contact Improvisation with Shibari, deepening our connections through movement, trust and shared discovery. Limited tickets available https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-stf0y-20261016183000

sessions.title: Bound & Unbound Weekend, Autumn Edition: Exploring the Edges of Contact Improvisation
sessions.details: Anatomie Studio, Arch 113 Unit 17 Station Passage, London, SE15 2JR • A three day intensive with Jamus Wood & Rick Nodine. Contact Improvisation, Shibari & BMC®. In this short Intensive we will take time to explore some of the territory at the edges of Contact Improvisation. Exploring aspects of the dance that often go unrecognised or don’t receive quality attention. Contact improvisation, led by Rick, will be at the heart of our practice and the ground of our shared language. We will spend the first part of our time together dropping into inner and outer awareness through Body Mind Centering® exercises and perspectives. The intention is to bring a felt sense of embodiment and a deep listening to our dance. Later we will draw inspiration from Shibari (Japanese Rope Play) with Jamus. Initially, we’ll focus on the sensations of our bodies in relation to one another, without ropes yet through physical limitations, exploring binding and unbinding, being held and being free. We will then introduce ropes, engaging in Ichinawa (one rope technique) to explore further the qualities of resistance and surrender, stillness and breath, gravity and flow. A key aspect of our workshop will be The Switch —a playful exploration of roles as we transition between being bound and binding with one’s body. We will slow down and zoom in, lingering in each role for some time. Also taking moments when neither of us are in a role. We will emphasise consent throughout our time together. Consent will be taught as a practice so that we can dance within our limits while finding and exploring our edges together. We’ll conclude each day with an exploratory jam, allowing space to integrate what we've explored and to express ourselves freely, weaving our individual journeys into a collective tapestry of creativity and joy. Join us in this supportive circle of explorers. Together, let’s celebrate the borders of Contact Improvisation with Shibari, deepening our connections through movement, trust and shared discovery. Limited tickets available https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-sz74o-20261017100000

sessions.title: Contact Improvisation Intensive With Rick Nodine: Training Ground, Autumn 26 Edition
sessions.details: Acland Burghley School Sports Centre, Churchill Road, London, NW5 1AN • London, Thursday October 29th to Sunday November 01st, 10h-17h In this intensive 4 days of study we will explore the ground that CI dances on. We will build our connect to earth, through the floor, with solo Somatics and simple but essential patterns that underpin the CI body. We will grow confidence in falling, rolling and inverting ourselves so that our partner dances can expand beyond habitual limits. Extended contact dancing guided by scores that demand connection through principles will guide us to the edges we need to work at. We will also reclaim the floor as playground for fresh explorations of the form and the pleasures of improvisation for its own sake. Time to explore, space to discover and days to build new skills will lead to questions and some answers about the dances you are looking for. https://bookwhen.com/ricknodine/e/ev-shsgr-20261029100000

sessions.details: Studio 5, Richard Hoggart Building, Goldsmiths, Lewisham Way, London, SE14 6NW • Contact Improvisation Workshop – Beginners and Beyond Back to Basics: Simple Skills for Complex Situations In this foundational workshop, we will explore some of the foundational qualities of locomoting, falling, sensing, and relaxing, to allow play in solo and in contact dances. Expect to a repertoire of states to apply in class and/or jamming in the wild! Bring things you would like to work on, too. As we will build parts of the day based around what you’re curious about in CI, using my experience in Contact Improvisation, Dance, and motor learning. https://bookwhen.com/cigoldsmiths/e/ev-sb3fv-20260124120000

sessions.title: 'Flow, Fall, Fly' CI Class with Zoe Solomons & Jam Soundscape from Gloria Yehilevsky DROP IN FOR THE JAM ALWAYS AVAILABLE
sessions.details: Studio 3, Richard Hoggart Building, London, Se14 6nw • A two hour Contact Improvisation class starting at 13:00 class followed by a jam at 15:00 finishing at 18:00. Flow, Fall, Fly In this class we will apply dynamic anatomical principles to help us find ease, flow and momentum in dancing. We will study patterns and tools that invite flow, to help us move between the ground and standing and make easy contact with partners. We will explore the gradient of flow vs solidity in service of comfortable weight sharing. This class references The Axis Syllabus, and Osteopathic understanding of the structure and function of the moving body for Contact Improvisation. ZOE may be creating a live improvised music score for class. Biography Zoë is a dancer/mother/teacher/Osteopath. Zoë has been been practicing and performing improvisation since 2000 and considers it a life practice as well as a professional skill. She has performed for Felix Ruckert, Tino Seghal and Simonetta Alessandri among others. Zoë qualified to teach Axis Syllabus universal motion principles in 2008, she combines this knowledge with anatomical understanding from her work as an osteopath in her teaching. She has taught at major dance centres and schools in the U.K. and internationally including, Earth Dance, Nomadic College, Emerging Hearts, The Place, Northern School of Contemporary Dance and many others. She is currently exploring Synaethesia in performance in her own work: The Perception Question See the website for more details on our pricing tiers.
