sessions.title: IN-SIGHT/IN-MOTION CI& FELDENKRAIS WORKSHOP with Simonetta Alessandri
sessions.details: Studio 3, Richard Hoggart Building, London, Se14 6nw • In-Sight / In-Motion Contact Improvisation & Feldenkrais We will explore how our vision influences our movement and how this reflects in our Contact Improvisation practice. Through a Feldenkrais exploration, we will investigate how eye movement influences whole-body coordination, orientation, and balance. Since vision and kinaesthetic information work together, sharpening how the brain processes visual information improves overall coordination, movement, and self-awareness. We will then explore the spectrum of visual possibilities in movement, ranging from closed eyes and soft peripheral vision to sustained eye contact and focused direct gazing. We will observe how these experiences alter how we navigate the confluence of inputs—from both the kinesthetic experience of physical contact and the various forms of visual information. We will draw upon this process to refine and deepen our practice, allowing intuitive responses to emerge spontaneously while also practicing specific technical solutions. Bio Simonetta is an Italian dance artist and a somatic educator based in London. She is an internationally recognised teacher that applies the Feldenkrais Method in dance and movement training as well as performance making. Her work is informed by more than 35 years of dancing, teaching and choreographing. Since 1991 Simonetta has been practicing, performing and teaching Contact improvisation. She kept Contact Improvisation alive in Rome for more than ten years through her classes and Jams. Currently she teaches at London Contemporary Dance School, Trinity Laban, and Goldsmiths University. She olds the Post Graduated Diploma from London Contemporary Dance School, she is qualified teacher of the Feldenkrais Method. She is a Fellow of the Higher Education Academy. She is one of the founders of CI@Goldsmiths, association of dance artists dedicated to the promotion and dissemination of Contact Improvisation in London and UK. https://bookwhen.com/cigoldsmiths/e/ev-sh1rl-20260131110000

sessions.title: Contact Improvisation WORKSHOP with Touchdown Dance
sessions.details: Goldsmiths University - room 154, Main Reception- Richard Hoggart Building, Lewisham Way, London, UK, SE14 6NW • Touchdown Dance ONE DAY workshop. Touchdown Dance workshops are uniquely devised to bring visually Impaired and sighted people together in movement and dance. Touchdown Dance workshops are an experience! Based in CI fundamentals devised by Steve Paxton and Anne Kilcoyne at Dartington in 1986 – 1994 and developed by the company in workshops, partnership projects and festivals over the decades. CI is based in the fusion of yoga, dance and Aikido and gymnastics. As a movement discipline, you discover a greater awareness of your body, posture and range of movement potential. Contact Improvisation involves working in physical contact with others to sense and feel our own weight, energy, and our ability to move together safely. Touchdown dance specialist workshop leaders are visually impaired and sighted, teaching through demonstration, audio description and consensual hands-on facilitation methods. We teach warm up and meet and greet exercises, that are fun and work with partnering and group explorations. The aim is to deepen your trust in your ability to move through space, to gain improved orientation skills and confidence in joining participatory physical activity. We work with principles and specialist inclusive methods developed over many years of research since 1986 – see our book Inclusive Dance, the story of Touchdown Dance, published by Intellectbooks.com ACCESS -Touchdown Dance is experienced in providing accessible sessions, we have worked in collaboration with Action for Blind, Henshaws Society for the Blind,SENSE, and many other organisations, nationally and internationally. -Guide Dogs are welcome and there is a walking area around the building. FOR access support please contact Katy Dymoke and Holly Thomas at info@touchdowndance.co.uk or call Katy 07932038730, For more about us go to our website; www.touchdowndance.co.uk https://bookwhen.com/cigoldsmiths/e/ev-sg5hz-20260208110000

sessions.title: Earthflow" CI Class with Akeim Toussaint Buck with & Jam Soundscape from TBC DROP IN FOR THE JAM ALWAYS AVAILABLE
sessions.details: Room 154, Richard Hoggart Building, Goldsmiths, Lewisham Way, London, SE14 6NW • A two hour Contact Improvisation class starting at 13:00 class followed by a jam at 15:00 finishing at 18:00. Earthflow I call my classes Earthflow, as my intention in my physical practice is to feel connected to the ground whilst seeking to find flow and expansion in my movement. Expect some set material and improvisation stimuli to investigate and interrogate. This is an approach inspired by Contemporary and Caribbean Dance Elements, Release Technique, Animal Flow, Yoga, Elements of Hip Hop and Capoeira. Together we seek liberation and flow, strengthening our connection to the earth both physically and energetically, seeking to find elasticity in our movement. We'll creatively explore cyclical movement patterns and rhythms, travelling through the space, inviting curiosity, accuracy, breath and clarity. In an energetic and playful environment, we'll revitalise our senses, build strength, stamina and open our awareness as we move together as a community. Akeim Toussaint Buck is an award-winning interdisciplinary performer and maker, born in Jamaica and raised in England. Founder of Toussaint To Move, his work blends dance, voice, and multiple art forms to explore socio-political realities through visceral, participatory performance grounded in improvisation and holistic movement practice. See more details on www.toussainttomove.com See the website for more details on our pricing tiers. sessions.title: 'BRAIDING BASS, BODY & SPACE' CI class with Nathaniel Parchment & Jam Soundscape from CYAN
sessions.details: Studio 3, Richard Hoggart Building, London, Se14 6nw • 'BRAIDING BASS, BODY & SPACE' This CLASS offers a generative warm-up and preparation for the contact improvisation jam, creating space for embodied exploration that honours non-hierarchical ways of knowing and being. Drawing on indigenous onto-epistemologies that recognize the intelligence and agency of all bodies—human and more-than-human—we'll engage with movement practices that dissolve boundaries between self and other, internal and external, doing and being. Through somatic investigations and light movement scores we'll attune to the subtle resonances that connect us: the rhythms of our own bodies - breath and heartbeat, the pulse of the collective, the vibration of sound and the sentient space we share. This approach invites us into a liberatory mode of attention where sensation, impulse, and communication flow freely with curiosity and play. Bio Nathaniel is a London-based dance artist whose interests lie at the intersections of embodied enquiry, African diasporic and queer social dance cultures, global body-mind practices and performance. In 2023 he graduated from the MA in Creative Practice: Dance Professional programme, delivered by Trinity Laban in partnership with Independent Dance (ID) and Siobhan Davies Studios, joining ID as Co-Director in the same year. Nathaniel is currently working as a freelance teacher, producer and choreographer, developing research into decolonising dance pedagogies and reparative performance research. He is part of a dance and music collective at K5 Studios, Harringay Warehouse District and runs the regular Global Majority Underscore workshop with Siobhan Davies Studios Co-Artistic Director Annie Lok at Pembroke House, South London. A two hour Contact Improvisation class starting at 13:00 class followed by a jam at 15:00 finishing at 18:00. See the website for more details on our pricing tiers. sessions.title: 'Dance Improvisation Practices that offer Resilience and Support in Difficult Times' Contact Improvisation WORKSHOP with Kirstie Simson
sessions.details: Laban Building - Car Park Studio, Creekside, London, SE8 3DZ • Dance Improvisation Practices that offer Resilience and Support in Difficult Times There is deep inspiration and rigour in a practice of improvisation that posits vulnerability at its heart. Developing the skills to be able to care for, engage, respect, and respond to that state of openness in oneself, others and our environment generates creative work that is powerful and transformative. Learning from a place of interconnectedness can give us the resilience and insight to face life's challenges and complexities. In this workshop Kirstie shares the movement practices and underlying philosophies she has developed over forty years of her committed involvement with dance improvisation as a ‘life- practice’ that has helped her negotiate life’s ups and downs. Integral with the physical practices she will elucidate how she views embodied practices as vitally important for developing the resilience to face into and respond to the challenging times we are living through. Having gone through recent life-threatening illness herself, Kirstie draws firsthand on how grounded dance practices can guide and sustain us as we face into life’s greatest challenges, and the life-affirming health benefits they offer us. Kirstie draws from her extensive experience of Contact Improvisation, dance techniques, the Alexander technique, Aikido, meditation, and her broad knowledge of improvisation in performance. She shares her work through facilitated exercises, open time for play and exploration, movement scores, observations, self-reflection, deep listening, discussion, and humour. Much of the work is experienced through partnering and connection with others, balanced with solo time for processing and reflection. https://bookwhen.com/cigoldsmiths/e/ev-sc968-20260308110000

sessions.title: BETWEEN SELF & OTHER CI Class with Laura Doehler & Jam Soundscape from CY-AN DROP IN FOR THE JAM ALWAYS AVAILABLE
sessions.details: Room 154, Richard Hoggart Building, Goldsmiths, Lewisham Way, London, SE14 6NW • A two hour Contact Improvisation class starting at 13:00 class followed by a jam at 15:00 finishing at 18:00. Between self and other In the shift from control to responsiveness, dancing begins; not by holding on, but by sensing what is and how your intention towards a certain kind of connection becomes a shared experience. We will inquire how awareness and intentional listening allow us to navigate touch and shared weight for an inclusive and consensual practice where partners, space, and our own desires and capacities to move are incorporated. Laura Doehler began her Contact Improvisation practice in 2004 with Kirstie Simson and soon connected to the wider international CI community, learning with pioneers such as Steve Paxton, Nancy Stark-Smith, Karen Nelson and many others who expanded the form into poetic, political, and somatic inquiries. She is a London-based dance artist having studied dance and performance making at Trinity Laban (2004) and Goldsmiths University (2012) and she is a recipient of the Trinity Laban scholarship. Her work as dance artist and director of Exit Map is firmly rooted in CI and improvisation. Her work is collaborative and participatory in nature and explores collective agency, scores of co-existence and care and proposes new socio-environmental relational practices such as outdoor dance events, rituals and embodied co-working spaces. In 2012 she co-founded Shared Practice with Anne-Gaelle Thiriot and Tania Soubry, which is an ongoing improvisation research project that she facilitates besides teaching in Higher Education at NCCA and independent workshops across Europe. Over the past years she connected to community organisations that work with CI and involve rural land practices. Recent residencies include Radical Inclusion and Architectures of Togetherness with Ponderosa e.V. in Germany, Catalysing Collective Action at The Place in London 2024 and soon with Larret En Mouvement as part of Larretude a research around Agentic Assemblages (FR). sessions.title: 'ATTUNEMENT & SUPPORT' Beginners and Beyond CI-Workshop with Thomas Kampe at Chisenhale Dance Space
sessions.details: Studio 3, Richard Hoggart Building, London, Se14 6nw • ATTUNEMENT & SUPPORT Please note. This workshop will be held at Chisenhale Dance Space Between 3pm and 7pm Chisenhale Dance Space https://share.google/PqMkGL9UFlA2OO1jm Beginners and Beyond CI Workshop with Thomas Kampe This workshop introduces Contact Improvisation through principles of the Feldenkrais Method®. Through gentle movement explorations and guided partner work, we develop sensitivity to touch, weight, and timing, discovering greater ease and spontaneity in our dancing. Feldenkrais-based Awareness Through Movement® supports clearer relationship with gravity in partnership situations including weight bearing, carrying and lifting situations. As we refine how we give, share and receive weight, we cultivate embodied listening, co-regulation, and creative responsiveness in more skills-oriented CI dancing. Open to beginners and experienced movers alike, this workshop invites curiosity, connection, and joyful shared movement. Thomas Kampe (PhD; UK/GER) lives in London and has worked with dance, theatre, performance and movement for the last 45 years. He has worked as performer, choreographer and director in Germany and Britain, and was Professor for Somatic Performance and Education at Bath Spa University, UK until 2022. Since the late 1980’s Thomas has taught somatic approaches towards Movement Education, Theatre and Contact Improvisation in different settings around the world. Thomas is a qualified teacher of the Feldenkrais Method® which forms a foundation for his teaching of dance and movement. His numerous research writings and performance works have been published internationally. https://bookwhen.com/cigoldsmiths/e/ev-s4dop-20260321150000

sessions.title: "SENSATIONAL MOVES": 9 -12 April . 4 DAY INTENSIVE Feldenkrais, Movement Exploration & CI with Simonetta Alessandri & Thomas Kampe
sessions.details: Room 154, Richard Hoggart Building, Goldsmiths, Lewisham Way, London, SE14 6NW • 9 -12 April 4 day Intensive exploring somatic dance practice, Feldenkrais Method and Contact Improvisation. In this four-day workshop, we combine somatic movement principles of the Feldenkrais Method® with dance exploration & Contact Improvisation practice. In the morning sessions we will be using ‘Awareness Through Movement’® lessons and touch-based explorations. We will focus on solo explorations and will connect with our core and imagination, deepening our felt sense of gravity and grounding. Through Dance Improvisational practice we will develop our observation skills and individual creative agency to integrate our embodied discoveries, fostering creative risk-taking and playfulness. In the afternoon sessions we will expand these explorations into the practice of Contact Improvisation to enhance our ability to interact and communicate expressively and playfully with ease and clarity. Drawing on our felt sense and somatic attunement through touch and gravity, we will revisit and refine Contact Improvisation skills and creative vocabularies, such as weight exchange, balance, support and lifts with a partner and in small ensemble situations. Each day we will take time to discuss or write reflectively about our personal and somatic discoveries. The Feldenkrais Method®, developed by Dr. Moshe Feldenkrais (1904-1984) is a gentle, embodied process focused on self-discovery, embodied thinking, creative wellbeing, and functional improvement. It invites participants to slow down, listen to internal sensations, and create new neural pathways that support creative choice making in action. This workshop is open to anyone interested in deepening their dance or movement practice. Thomas and Simonetta have been teaching Feldenkrais-informed dance classes around the globe for over 20 years and are world-leading researchers and artists in the field. Dr.Thomas Kampe (PhD) (he/him) has worked as a performer, artist, researcher and somatic educator across the globe for 40 years and was ‘Professor of Somatic Performance and Education’ at Bath Spa University (UK) between 2012 and 2022. Thomas initially trained in visual art and dance and is a teacher of the Feldenkrais Method ® which forms a rich foundation for his teaching, arts practice and research. He is interested in critical somatic arts legacies and his writings and performance works have been published internationally. He was editor of the Feldenkrais Research Journal Vol. 6 ‘Practices of Freedom: The Feldenkrais Method & Creativity’ in 2019, and recently co-edited JDSP Vol. 13.1 & 2 (2022): ‘Embodying Eco-Consciousness: Somatics, Aesthetic Practice and Social Action’. Simonetta Alessandri is an Italian dance artist & somatic educator based in London. She applies the Feldenkrais Method in dance & movement training and in performance making. She teaches at Trinity Laban, Goldsmiths University and London Contemporary Dance School. Her work is informed by more than 30 years of dancing, teaching and choreographing. Her choreography has been for dance companies, student pieces, large scale opera, improvised performance, site specific and movement direction for theatre. She was one of the few dance practitioners in Italy who began working with CI in the 90’s. She has been a guest teacher in Germany, Colombia, UK, Norway, Israel, France, Switzerland, Italy, Poland, Spain and Taiwan. She obtained the Post Graduate Diploma at London Contemporary Dance School; she is a qualified teacher of the Feldenkrais Method and she holds the Teacher Certificate of the Royal Academy of Dance. She is a founder member of CI@Goldsmiths London. https://bookwhen.com/cigoldsmiths/e/ev-swh5j-20260409100000

Thomas initially trained in visual art and dance and is a teacher of the Feldenkrais Method ® which forms a rich foundation for his teaching, arts practice and research. He is interested in critical somatic arts legacies and his writings and performance works have been published internationally. He was editor of the Feldenkrais Research Journal Vol. 6 ‘Practices of Freedom: The Feldenkrais Method & Creativity’ in 2019, and recently co-edited JDSP Vol. 13.1 & 2 (2022): ‘Embodying Eco-Consciousness: Somatics, Aesthetic Practice and Social Action’. Simonetta Alessandri is an Italian dance artist & somatic educator based in London. She applies the Feldenkrais Method in dance & movement training and in performance making. She teaches at Trinity Laban, Goldsmiths University and London Contemporary Dance School. Her work is informed by more than 30 years of dancing, teaching and choreographing. Her choreography has been for dance companies, student pieces, large scale opera, improvised performance, site specific and movement direction for theatre. She was one of the few dance practitioners in Italy who began working with CI in the 90’s. She has been a guest teacher in Germany, Colombia, UK, Norway, Israel, France, Switzerland, Italy, Poland, Spain and Taiwan. She obtained the Post Graduate Diploma at London Contemporary Dance School; she is a qualified teacher of the Feldenkrais Method and she holds the Teacher Certificate of the Royal Academy of Dance. She is a founder member of CI@Goldsmiths London. https://bookwhen.com/cigoldsmiths/e/ev-swh5j-20260410100000

sessions.title: "SENSATIONAL MOVES": 9 -12 April . 4 DAY INTENSIVE Feldenkrais, Movement Exploration & CI with Simonetta Alessandri & Thomas Kampe
sessions.details: Room 154, Richard Hoggart Building, Goldsmiths, Lewisham Way, London, SE14 6NW • 9 -12 April 4 day Intensive exploring somatic dance practice, Feldenkrais Method and Contact Improvisation. In this four-day workshop, we combine somatic movement principles of the Feldenkrais Method® with dance exploration & Contact Improvisation practice. In the morning sessions we will be using ‘Awareness Through Movement’® lessons and touch-based explorations. We will focus on solo explorations and will connect with our core and imagination, deepening our felt sense of gravity and grounding. Through Dance Improvisational practice we will develop our observation skills and individual creative agency to integrate our embodied discoveries, fostering creative risk-taking and playfulness. In the afternoon sessions we will expand these explorations into the practice of Contact Improvisation to enhance our ability to interact and communicate expressively and playfully with ease and clarity. Drawing on our felt sense and somatic attunement through touch and gravity, we will revisit and refine Contact Improvisation skills and creative vocabularies, such as weight exchange, balance, support and lifts with a partner and in small ensemble situations. Each day we will take time to discuss or write reflectively about our personal and somatic discoveries. The Feldenkrais Method®, developed by Dr. Moshe Feldenkrais (1904-1984) is a gentle, embodied process focused on self-discovery, embodied thinking, creative wellbeing, and functional improvement. It invites participants to slow down, listen to internal sensations, and create new neural pathways that support creative choice making in action. This workshop is open to anyone interested in deepening their dance or movement practice. Thomas and Simonetta have been teaching Feldenkrais-informed dance classes around the globe for over 20 years and are world-leading researchers and artists in the field. Dr.Thomas Kampe (PhD) (he/him) has worked as a performer, artist, researcher and somatic educator across the globe for 40 years and was ‘Professor of Somatic Performance and Education’ at Bath Spa University (UK) between 2012 and 2022. Thomas initially trained in visual art and dance and is a teacher of the Feldenkrais Method ® which forms a rich foundation for his teaching, arts practice and research. He is interested in critical somatic arts legacies and his writings and performance works have been published internationally. He was editor of the Feldenkrais Research Journal Vol. 6 ‘Practices of Freedom: The Feldenkrais Method & Creativity’ in 2019, and recently co-edited JDSP Vol. 13.1 & 2 (2022): ‘Embodying Eco-Consciousness: Somatics, Aesthetic Practice and Social Action’. Simonetta Alessandri is an Italian dance artist & somatic educator based in London. She applies the Feldenkrais Method in dance & movement training and in performance making. She teaches at Trinity Laban, Goldsmiths University and London Contemporary Dance School. Her work is informed by more than 30 years of dancing, teaching and choreographing. Her choreography has been for dance companies, student pieces, large scale opera, improvised performance, site specific and movement direction for theatre. She was one of the few dance practitioners in Italy who began working with CI in the 90’s. She has been a guest teacher in Germany, Colombia, UK, Norway, Israel, France, Switzerland, Italy, Poland, Spain and Taiwan. She obtained the Post Graduate Diploma at London Contemporary Dance School; she is a qualified teacher of the Feldenkrais Method and she holds the Teacher Certificate of the Royal Academy of Dance. She is a founder member of CI@Goldsmiths London. https://bookwhen.com/cigoldsmiths/e/ev-swh5j-20260411100000

sessions.title: 'From Floor to Flying' CI Class with Josh Jones & Jam Soundscape from Michael Sebastian DROP IN FOR THE JAM ALWAYS AVAILABLE
sessions.details: Studio 3, Richard Hoggart Building, London, Se14 6nw • A two hour Contact Improvisation class starting at 13:00 class followed by a jam at 15:00 finishing at 18:00. From Floor to Flying This 2-hour class explores the relationship between floor and sky through contemporary floorwork and contact improvisation. We begin with attunement - arriving into body, space, and shared awareness. Through head-tail connectivity, spirals, yielding, and momentum, we develop an intelligent relationship with the ground. In my approach, the floor is our first partner. What we learn here - how to pour weight, how to trust support, how to yield - translates directly into dancing with another body. In flight and base relationships, your base becomes your floor. Through their support, you can feel the earth again. We work with pouring weight rather than placing it - allowing time for invitations to be sensed and received. Instead of making something happen, we practice allowing something to emerge. Invitations move both ways, and through listening and timing we find shared consent in motion. Open to experienced movers and confident beginners comfortable with weight and level changes. BIO Josh Jones is a movement educator and facilitator working at the meeting point of floorwork, somatics, and contact improvisation. With a background in contemporary dance training and years of immersion in CI communities, festivals, and research spaces internationally, his practice bridges technical precision with embodied listening. He has studied release-based floorwork, partnering, and somatic approaches that inform his progressive pathway from ground to flight. Josh has led workshops, retreats, and intensives across the UK, Europe, and India, supporting movers in developing clarity, adaptability, and trust in shared weight. He is the creator of the Lean In projects - including Lean In Nature and From Floor to Flying - immersive training spaces that explore the floor as our first partner, cultivating skill, sensitivity, and human connection through movement. See the website for more details on our pricing tiers. sessions.title: 'Meeting, Connecting ,Playing' Contact Improvisation WORKSHOP with Lucia Walker
sessions.details: Studio 3, Richard Hoggart Building, London, Se14 6nw • Meeting, Connecting ,Playing Meeting yourself, other dancers and the physical forces of this planet with more simplicity and depth. Connecting with more ease Playing with more presence, consent and freedom. Balancing the excitement of moving and connecting with the calm of receptivity and co-ordination. Working with directing our attention we will continue to refine the ability to be present with ourselves, our partners and the dance that arises. Guided explorations will increase our discernment and enjoyment of the experiences of momentum, pressure, support, weightbearing and movement. We will practise ‘not doing’ some of what arises automatically in order to access more possibility in our responses to stimuli and the creativity to be found with ease in the present. We will also learn to notice and support the flow of choices and co-incidences that allow us to enjoy and influence the dance. The workshop will include quiet and subtle work as well as energetic and playful movement. Each participant is invited to participate according to their own ability and energy. It will be suited to experienced dancers and those newer to the form. Lucia Walker I am based in Wales and work internationally as an Alexander teacher and Movement artist. I have been dancing all my life and involved with AT and Contact Improvisation for 40 years. I continue to practise, perform and teach Contact Improvisation and Instant Composition, exploring how to easily enter a creative state of presence. I also teach AT to groups and individuals as well as training teachers. Enjoyment of the sense of vitality and well-being that arose from learning Alexander Technique, and a desire to support people to be more fully themselves and to express themselves more easily in their chosen life activities, stimulated me to train as a teacher. I met Contact Improvisation in 1982, studying with Steve Paxton, Nancy Stark Smith, Kirstie Simson and practising through teaching with Jointwork Dance Group and others.Both AT and CI work with principles that enable us to consciously co-operate with our human design, able to be both active and receptive, calm and lively. I continue to practise, perform and teach Contact Improvisation and Instant Composition, exploring how to easily enter a creative state of presence. I facilitate regularly for Independent Dance (London), Forgotten Angle Theatre Collaborative (FATC) at Ebhudlweni rural arts centre South Africa) and participate in collaborative projects as well as continuing solo work.Lifelong learning is supported by teaching people from a wide range of ages, abilities and cultural backgrounds. I love the way movement exploration, awareness practices and play can provide both a method of self-care and a ‘common language’ which lies beneath different cultural experiences and styles of dance https://bookwhen.com/cigoldsmiths/e/ev-shb2z-20260425110000

sessions.title: SKILLS FOR JAMMING CI Class with & Jam Soundscape
sessions.details: Room 154, Richard Hoggart Building, Goldsmiths, Lewisham Way, London, SE14 6NW • A two hour Contact Improvisation class starting at 13:00 class followed by a jam at 15:00 finishing at 18:00 Contact Improvisation Class with Caroline Waters "Skills for Jamming" Referencing Steve Paxtons 'Material for the Spine", the class will focus on fundamentals of Ci that underpin the dancing which also draws from ki aikido. We will explore moving from a more relaxed and centred sense as we move in and out of contact with each other and the floor. Listening, sensing, following the flow, supporting and negotiating through touch the various exits and entrances to the dance. working in solo, duet and more, honing our listening and responding skills for dancing.. Caroline Waters is a Freelance Performer, Organiser and Arts Educator currently based in Brighton UK. Mainly drawing from CI and New Dance practices, She teaches, performs and directs in various situations ranging from the performic to the therapeutic, working with both professionals and non-professionals. The work often focuses on collaboration and working with other Art forms, which includes experience of working with a range of non-theatre spaces as a locus for performance. She taught Contextual Practice for 11 years at Dartington College or Arts and for the past 14 years was an associate Lecturer in CI at Chichester University UK. sessions.title: DYNAMIC CONNECTION Contact Improvisation WORKSHOP with Mark Rietema
sessions.details: Goldsmiths University - room 154, Main Reception- Richard Hoggart Building, Lewisham Way, London, UK, SE14 6NW • Dynamic Connection - Contact Improvisation Workshop Sat 9th May 11am-5pm For this day workshop we'll set the ground for a more explorative and dynamic dance with ourselves and with others. Early developmental movement patterns, movement puzzles and partner explorations will bring us into our dancing body, with a specific focus on differentiating and connecting our center and periphery. We’ll spend time with our fascia and the felt sense of our connective tissue, and how this shapes our dance and keeps us present in the unknown during lifts. Particularly I’d like to slow lifts down, so that we can override our fear responses from a place of groundedness, and explore the subtle shifts of tone and placement that offer new directions and possibilities. From this detailed and attentive place we’ll find our way back into a more embodied improvisational space through scores and practices where technique can soften, and where dancing feels grounded, safe and genuinely playful. The work draws on the gentle, listening qualities of Body-Mind Centering® as a foundation for more dynamic exploration, and is open to all levels. Mark Rietema teaches Somatics and CI in London and abroad, and offers workshops, lectures and group facilitation around embodiment, dance and mental health in places such as King’s College, the NHS and grassroots groups. He works as a psychotherapist and somatic movement educator, having studied Process Oriented Psychology and Body-Mind Centering®. His previous background is in community arts projects and performances (UK, US) with an MA in Community Arts from Goldsmiths. He teaches as faculty for Embody Move UK, Institut Prozessarbeit Deutschland, and as a guest teacher in various institutions. www.markrietema.com https://bookwhen.com/cigoldsmiths/e/ev-sim4j-20260509110000

sessions.title: Monday Contact Improv Jam
sessions.details: Rose Lipman Building, 43 De Beauvoir Road, London, N1 • This weekly jam invites movers to jam together for a regular practice. Warming-up: Marie Chabert will facilitate the warming-up before the jam. The aim is for participants to arrive into their body, tune in, connect to themselves, to others and to the space around. It brings people into their senses and embodied awareness. It is not a class and doesn't give the same technical tools and principles as a class. Classes are held every other week and will give you these skills. Jam The emphasis of the jam is to explore movement while being in physical contact with other movers. Participants are encouraged to experience it as an embodied research and a practice where everyone can bring their own research, interest and physicality into the space. Jamming is the best way to try out, take risks, learn from each other, observe, connect, be surprised, practice setting boundaries, dance and play with people we may not know or expect to dance with. Participants are asked to take responsibility for themselves. Levels and abilities: The space welcomes professional dancers, amateurs, seasoned contact improvisers and beginners who took a few classes beforehand, people from all backgrounds and abilities. It requires every participants to take responsibility for their own bodies, so if you need extra support or a carer or someone to look after you then you can bring them for free. Note for beginners: the jam is not guided. The facilitator is there to hold the space but doesn't guide the jam. What is Contact Improvisation (CI)? Contact Improvisation is movement research that explores the physical principles of touch, momentum, and shared weight in relation to gravity. It involves a constant dialogue between two or more movers who respond to each other’s movements in real time, often maintaining physical contact but also playing with proximity and space. This practice emphasises spontaneity, trust, and mutual awareness, allowing dancers to create unique movement for each encounter. Connect to the community If you want to join the CI Monday Jam WhatsApp group, click on this link: https://chat.whatsapp.com/Iivbz8LzDvK5lGkiitQgcH (when you join please introduce yourself so we know who's in the chat) More events on mariechabert.com https://bookwhen.com/mariechabert/e/ev-sllyb-20260202183000

Contact Improvisation is movement research that explores the physical principles of touch, momentum, and shared weight in relation to gravity. It involves a constant dialogue between two or more movers who respond to each other’s movements in real time, often maintaining physical contact but also playing with proximity and space. sessions.title: Monday Contact Improv : Contemplative Practice into Silent Jam
sessions.details: Rose Lipman Building, 43 De Beauvoir Road, London, N1 • This weekly jam invites movers to jam together for a regular practice. For this one, no late commers will be admitted after 6.40pm! The Contemplative Dance Practice was developed by the American dancer, choreographer and Buddhist Barbara Dilley in the 70's, 80's. There will be a clear structure: -Opening circle and explanation of the structure -Sitting Meditation (15 min), -Personal Awareness Practice (15 min) -Open Space into Silent Jam (until 8.15pm) -Close-up circle (finishes at 8.30pm) Jam The emphasis of the jam is to explore movement while being in physical contact with other movers. sessions.title: **Contact Improv & Philosophy Playshop: DANCING... GENDER-LESS!**
sessions.details: Chisenhale Dance Space, 64-84 Chisenhale Road, London, E3 5QZ • A playshop on undoing gender in and through contact improvisation This Playshop (workshop/playful lab) is led by Marie Chabert and Dr Noah Martin (Women's Touch meets the College of modern anxiety) How does gender come into your dance? Many of us experience tremendous liberation from the constraints of gender roles through contact improv; this playshop from Marie Chabert and Dr. Noah Martin will explore ways to bring that kind of freedom into the rest of your life and your relationships, as well as how we can open ourselves up to even greater self-expression and even deeper ways of relating to each other in the dance space. This playshop will involve: Movement games to help reveal ways that gender shapes patterns of movement and ways of relating Philosophical ideas to help us understand the role of gender in our lives Time for self-reflection and journaling …and of course, time to jam! https://bookwhen.com/mariechabert/e/ev-sk4q-20260314140000

Participants are encouraged to experience it as an embodied research and a practice where everyone can bring their own research, interest and physicality into the space. Jamming is the best way to try out, take risks, learn from each other, observe, connect, be surprised, practice setting boundaries, dance and play with people we may not know or expect to dance with. Participants are asked to take responsibility for themselves. Levels and abilities: The space welcomes professional dancers, amateurs, seasoned contact improvisers and beginners, people from all backgrounds and abilities. It requires every participants to take responsibility for their own bodies, so if you need extra support or a carer or someone to look after you then you can bring them for free. Note for beginners: the jam is not guided. The facilitator is there to hold the space but doesn't guide the jam. What is Contact Improvisation (CI)? Contact Improvisation is movement research that explores the physical principles of touch, momentum, and shared weight in relation to gravity. It involves a constant dialogue between two or more movers who respond to each other’s movements in real time, often maintaining physical contact but also playing with proximity and space. This practice emphasises spontaneity, trust, and mutual awareness, allowing dancers to create unique movement for each encounter. Event page: https://millco.co.uk/events/contact-improvisation-jam/ https://bookwhen.com/mariechabert/e/ev-sw6wq-20260316183000

sessions.title: **Women’s Touch : Community as a relational process with Marie Chabert & Manuela Albrecht**
sessions.details: Siobhan Davies Studios, 85 Saint George's Road, London, SE1 6ER • Embodiment - Community - Safe Space Develop self-enquiry and autonomy- Be in touch - Share impressions + experiences + food For this edition Marie Chabert is inviting the dance practitioner Manuela Albrecht to share her beautiful research on mycelium and inter-connectivity. Manuela and Marie will facilitate this Contact Impro workshop/laboratory exploring how community forms through relation and difference. Weaving between movement scores and moments of shared reflection, participants are invited to experience connection in and out of contact using yarns, a soft fibrous material used for crochet. We will open up questions and investigations such as: -How do I enter and inhabit a group? -What helps me feel connected without erasing difference? -Is community something we belong to, or something we continuously make? Work unfolds through solo and group scores, supporting attentive connection, experimentation, and self-enquiry. The space intends to be safe, consent-based, and accessible to all. No prior experience with Contact Improvisation is required. Rough schedule (may change slightly) 11.30am-12pm – Welcome tea & chat (meeting each other + time & space to land) 12pm –1.30pm – Intro to Consent, solo warm-up and collective embodied explorations 1.30-2.30pm – Lunchtime: food sharing 2.30-5.30pm – Group score playing with the yarns. Small group reflection: community as a relational process Witnessing, writing, sharing and integrating Who is this for? Those who identify as women and non-binary interested in bringing more awareness to physical interactions in dancing and in Contact Improvisation It welcomes all levels and can be applied to other practices than Contact Improvisation. In the past people were able to apply it to dance therapy, theatre, psychology... What is Women's Touch? Women’s touch is an event curated by Marie Chabert, that offers a space for those who identify as women and non-binary to explore movement practices that are mainly based on touch (Contact Improvisation, somatic practices, improvisation) in a safe environment. It aims to empower women to claim their space and redefine it within a framework that challenges prevailing patriarchal norms. It offers both a place to embody and share our experiences verbally. The intention is to foster a supportive environment, where women and non-binary participants feel safe to take risks, be vulnerable, and share their humanity, free from the pressure of dominant dynamics. Cultivating such spaces requires trust, shared language, and mutual care. It allows us not just to survive, but to thrive in connection. Manuela's bio: I’m a Brazilian interdisciplinary movement artist, facilitator, performer, and PhD researcher working at the intersection of dance, theatre, community, and micro-politics. My practice explores movement as a way to reflect on the urgencies of our time. I’m especially drawn to collaborative processes and to disrupt colonial legacies, the normative, and stay radically tender, embodied, and real. https://manualbrecht5.wixsite.com/manualbrecht/ If you have financial difficulties and want to take part, please get in touch. photo by Sol Ji Park https://bookwhen.com/mariechabert/e/ev-sv4ry-20260510233000

sessions.title: Moving Encounters - An experiential workshop for therapists exploring embodied relational awareness
sessions.details: St Augustine's Church Hall, Langdon Park Road, London, N6 5QG • About the workshop A one-day experiential workshop exploring embodied awareness and relational presence through structured movement-based inquiry. This is a guided and contained space for working with attention, sensation, movement, and relation as they arise in real time. The emphasis is on direct experience rather than interpretation, performance, or technique. The day is structured, but not prescriptive, allowing space for both individual exploration and relational processes with others. The atmosphere of the day The workshop provides a structured experiential setting for exploring how awareness is organised in the body and in relation. At times the work will be internally focused and quiet. At other times it will involve movement in relation to others, with clear frameworks supporting consent, choice, and pacing throughout. Throughout the day, the intention is to stay close to what is actually happening in experience, moment by moment. Approaches informing the work This workshop draws on principles from: Body-Mind CenteringⓇ — somatic and developmental awareness of the body Authentic Movement — witnessed movement and reflective awareness Contact Improvisation — weight, responsiveness, and relational movement These are not taught as techniques, but used as reference points within structured experiential inquiry. What will be explored Across the day, the work will include: -attention to sensation, impulse, and movement -how awareness shifts between self and other -relational contact and distance -witnessing and being witnessed -patterns of responsiveness in interaction -how experience is received and registered in the body Participation is always invitational, and there is space to observe or pause at any time. Who this is for This workshop is open to therapists, counsellors, psychotherapists, and practitioners working in relational or embodied contexts. It is designed for those interested in exploring embodied awareness as part of their reflective practice, particularly those curious about how bodily experience shapes presence, attention, and relational responsiveness. No previous movement or dance experience is required. What this is not This is not: a dance or performance workshop a training in any therapeutic modality a group therapy or personal process group a space for psychological interpretation or treatment It is an experiential workshop for embodied inquiry and relational exploration. Reflection and integration There will be structured opportunities to reflect verbally on experience during the day. Reflection is offered as a way of noticing and articulating experience, rather than interpreting or analysing it. Participants are encouraged to consider how the work may relate to their own professional practice. About the facilitator This workshop is facilitated by Mark Rietema, a UKCP-registered Psychotherapist, trained in Process Work, and Somatic Movement Therapist (BMCA) with a background spanning clinical practice, somatic education, and movement facilitation. Alongside a clinical practice that has included extensive work within NHS mental health services, Mark teaches and facilitates in a range of professional and academic contexts, including Embody-Move UK, Institut Prozessarbeit Deutschland, and King's College London, where they contributed to research on arts and wellbeing in higher education. He teaches Body-Mind Centering within official BMCⓇ trainings in the UK, Spain, and Italy. Mark also teaches Contact Improvisation nationally and internationally, and is part of the organising team of the London Contact Festival and CI@Goldsmiths. This direct grounding in CI practice informs the relational and movement dimensions of the workshop. A background in community arts (MA, Goldsmiths) and experience facilitating internationally - including in the USA, Palestine, and Germany - continues to shape an approach that is both rigorous and attentive to the lived complexity of group work. Mark will be supported throughout the day by Sole de la Hoz, a Contact Improvisation teacher and trainee psychotherapist in Process Oriented Psychology, with a professional background in dance. https://bookwhen.com/markrietema/e/ev-s2y7g-20260621100000

sessions.title: Introduction to CI
sessions.details:
Walworth Living Room, All Saints Hall, Surrey Square, London SE17 2JU, UK • https://www.eventbrite.com/e/1977172768976?aff=oddtdtcreator

In this gentle workshop, we’ll offer simple, playful ways to connect through movement. Contact Improvisation, also called CI, can include touch, sharing weight, or dynamic motion, but we will keep everything light, optional, and based on comfort.

We want this event to be accessible to all abilities, ages and backgrounds.

We will offer ways to mobilise your body to begin with which everyone adapts as needed. It is important that you choose how you want to move, and who you want to move with.

sessions.title: "SENSATIONAL MOVES": 9 -12 April . 4 DAY INTENSIVE Feldenkrais, Movement Exploration & CI with Simonetta Alessandri & Thomas Kampe
sessions.details: Room 154, Richard Hoggart Building, Goldsmiths, Lewisham Way, London, SE14 6NW • 9 -12 April 4 day Intensive exploring somatic dance practice, Feldenkrais Method and Contact Improvisation. In this four-day workshop, we combine somatic movement principles of the Feldenkrais Method® with dance exploration & Contact Improvisation practice. In the morning sessions we will be using ‘Awareness Through Movement’® lessons and touch-based explorations. We will focus on solo explorations and will connect with our core and imagination, deepening our felt sense of gravity and grounding. Through Dance Improvisational practice we will develop our observation skills and individual creative agency to integrate our embodied discoveries, fostering creative risk-taking and playfulness. In the afternoon sessions we will expand these explorations into the practice of Contact Improvisation to enhance our ability to interact and communicate expressively and playfully with ease and clarity. Drawing on our felt sense and somatic attunement through touch and gravity, we will revisit and refine Contact Improvisation skills and creative vocabularies, such as weight exchange, balance, support and lifts with a partner and in small ensemble situations. Each day we will take time to discuss or write reflectively about our personal and somatic discoveries. The Feldenkrais Method®, developed by Dr. Moshe Feldenkrais (1904-1984) is a gentle, embodied process focused on self-discovery, embodied thinking, creative wellbeing, and functional improvement. It invites participants to slow down, listen to internal sensations, and create new neural pathways that support creative choice making in action. This workshop is open to anyone interested in deepening their dance or movement practice. Thomas and Simonetta have been teaching Feldenkrais-informed dance classes around the globe for over 20 years and are world-leading researchers and artists in the field. Dr.Thomas Kampe (PhD) (he/him) has worked as a performer, artist, researcher and somatic educator across the globe for 40 years and was ‘Professor of Somatic Performance and Education’ at Bath Spa University (UK) between 2012 and 2022. Thomas initially trained in visual art and dance and is a teacher of the Feldenkrais Method ® which forms a rich foundation for his teaching, arts practice and research. He is interested in critical somatic arts legacies and his writings and performance works have been published internationally. He was editor of the Feldenkrais Research Journal Vol. 6 ‘Practices of Freedom: The Feldenkrais Method & Creativity’ in 2019, and recently co-edited JDSP Vol. 13.1 & 2 (2022): ‘Embodying Eco-Consciousness: Somatics, Aesthetic Practice and Social Action’. Simonetta Alessandri is an Italian dance artist & somatic educator based in London. She applies the Feldenkrais Method in dance & movement training and in performance making. She teaches at Trinity Laban, Goldsmiths University and London Contemporary Dance School. Her work is informed by more than 30 years of dancing, teaching and choreographing. Her choreography has been for dance companies, student pieces, large scale opera, improvised performance, site specific and movement direction for theatre. She was one of the few dance practitioners in Italy who began working with CI in the 90’s. She has been a guest teacher in Germany, Colombia, UK, Norway, Israel, France, Switzerland, Italy, Poland, Spain and Taiwan. She obtained the Post Graduate Diploma at London Contemporary Dance School; she is a qualified teacher of the Feldenkrais Method and she holds the Teacher Certificate of the Royal Academy of Dance. She is a founder member of CI@Goldsmiths London. https://bookwhen.com/cigoldsmiths/e/ev-swh5j-20260409100000

sessions.details:
Saint Pierre de Frugie, France, near Limoges •

https://www.exitmap.org/vu


Intensive

Led by Laura Doehler with Asaf Bachrach and Harriet Roberts
How does the image we create, the identity we hold, meet the call for change?
Being visible in the tensions and negotiations that we experience, we practice
Contact Improvisation for embodied experiences to make sense of the everyday.

sessions.title: Weaving Spatial Support into the Body, Laura Doehler
sessions.details: Rockingham Community Centre, Falmouth Rd, London SE1 6PD, UK • In this workshop we will explore how our weight always is supported by default and acts as support simply through being in space and sensing our own physical space. We strip back expectations around support to uncover how a changing physical landscape of body and space create a container that at times carries us through space and at other times means we become a carrier or both. Our intention is to challenge our ideas around support so we won't get locked into carrying or loading and acquire greater skills in falling through letting go and expansion. We will probe spatial anchoring by tapping into the expansive qualities that space offers, find lightness and the ability to redirect oneself. These processes shall support a greater freedom in our dancing inside and outside the studio as we reckon with our ideas and expectations around support.

sessions.title: London CI Weekender - 2-days of Contact Improvisation, Exploring the concepts of 'reaching and meeting' and 'elements of the jam' w/ Josh Jones and Lily Antonia
sessions.details:
Willesden, London, UK •

London CI Weekender
A 2-day Contact Improvisation Workshop

https://dandelion.events/e/londonciweekender

Whats on offer :

A weekend of movement, exploration, co-collaboration and connection. Diving into the fundamentals & philosophy of Contact Improvisation. The London CI weekender offers space and guidance to engage with the practice, tune into the body & develop the skills that serve us both in the dance and in daily life. Bringing shared inquiry to the foreground to drop deeper through the layers of what is possible through CI.

Each day has a distinct focus, and participants are welcome to attend either day or the full weekend.

Come deepen your connection to the ground, space, and others while gaining tools for (contact) improvisation, jamming, and attuned awareness through the joy of movement.

Saturday: Reaching & Meeting

How can we meet the earth, meet ourselves and meet another?

How is it to be met?

Investigating and understanding mutual meeting as a core part of the dance.

Reaching as a tool to transform into oscillating expansive movers. To expand awareness and expand the ability of the body. Coming away from collapse and into lightness. Effortlessly working with gravity and momentum.

The day will include technical insights and the approach is practice as research. Allowing ourselves to be informed by the dance - discover how to move functionally and fluidly, open to all that's available in the present. Through solo, partner & group exercises we’ll explore reaching and meeting as a shared point of attention.

This day offers a grounding in presence, deep listening, and mutual direction.

Sunday: Elements of the Jam


On Sunday, we move into the world of the jam. We’ll explore how to enter and exit dances, navigate shared space, and respond to each unfolding moment. Through structured improvisation scores and instant compositions, we’ll build tools for the jam, develop adaptability, and co-create through group improvisation.

A container for emergence and spontaneity.

Meet your facilitators

Lily Antonia (She/Her)

Dance & Movement Artist | Facilitator

Lily is a contemporary dance artist, specialising in contact improvisation. She has been performing, teaching and choreographing internationally since graduating from Trinity Laban Conservatoire of Music and Dance. Working with companies Wired Aerial Theatre, Mad Dogs Dance Theatre, and Company Chameleon. Lily is co-director of contemporary dance and live art company UMAMI-i!-MOVEMENT and co-founder of Contact Improv MCR.

Her approach to CI blends playful exploration with technical clarity. Inviting curiosity, challenging potential and nurturing individual creativity - Lily invites dancers to bring an aliveness to the present. Creating spaces where dancers can listen, trust, and move with intention and spontaneity.

“To cultivate a state of being - in readiness without anticipation... we open ourselves to

all possibilities"

Joshua Jarvis Jones (He/They)

Dancer | Facilitator | Choreographer

Josh is a contemporary dancer and facilitator whose practice is rooted in emotional exploration and embodied connection. Following a time of personal loss, he found dance—especially contact improvisation—as a vital tool for healing, grounding, and rediscovering relationships with self and others.

Trained at the Northern School of Contemporary Dance, Josh has performed internationally with artists like Ricardo Buscarini and Madeline Shan, exploring themes of intimacy and identity. His deep engagement with contact improvisation bridges technique and relational psychology, and he shares this through workshops, labs, and festivals worldwide.

Josh co founded *Lean_In*, a nomadic movement series that has grown into the annual retreat *Lean_In_Nature*, inviting people into co-creative, embodied research. He has taught at CI hubs and circus events across Europe. His work invites participants into a dance of authenticity, where movement becomes a meeting place for truth and transformation.

INCLUSIVE AND SAFE SPACE

We are committed to creating an inclusive and safe space for all participants. This workshop is designed to be accessible to all and participants are welcome to let us know in advance about specific access needs.

Who’s the workshop for

Open to all levels. Whether you’re a seasoned pro or you've never done the practice before there is something for everyone. No previous dance experience required just a willingness to explore & create.

Where

Location will be given upon booking.

We will be dancing in the heart of Camberwell, the space has a kitchen available for use by participants, toilets and showers. Access to the hall is via a flight of stairs.

If you would like to attend but have financial concerns / are unable to purchase a ticket due to financial reasons please reach out - we will find the way with you.

This event is 18+

If you have any queries / questions / thoughts or just want to say hi please feel free to get in touch - leaninmovement123@gmail.com

https://dandelion.events/e/londonciweekender

sessions.title: Cubaneando saturday workshop
sessions.details: O2 Centre,The Venue Hall, O2 Centre,The Venue Hall, 255 Finchley Road, London, NW3 6LU, United Kingdom • ★SATURDAYS INFO: If you booked for Saturday you have already paid online Remember there are 10h & 12h Bundles available for £60 depending on your level. sessions.title: Casino Libre
sessions.details: Farr’s Dalston, 17-19 Dalston Ln,, London, E8 3DF, United Kingdom • Casino (Cuban Salsa) Partnerwork Classes Mondays | 7:00–11:00 PM Drop-in classes | 3 Levels Join us for a focused evening of Casino (Cuban Salsa) partnerwork, dedicated to building strong foundations, clean technique, and confident social dancing all rooted in authentic Cuban style. These drop-in classes are designed to support dancers at different stages, with three progressive levels running back-to-back so you can join exactly where you feel comfortable. Class Structure Intro: For beginners and those new to Casino. Learn the essential basics of Cuban partnerwork, connection, flow, and movement in a friendly, supportive environment. Improvers: For dancers with some experience. Strengthen your foundations, refine technique, and develop better flow, timing, and partner communication. Basics. For confident dancers ready to deepen their skills. Explore more complex partnerwork, musical interpretation, variation, and technique with a strong focus on clarity, control, and social usability. What to Expect Clear, structured teaching Strong emphasis on correct Casino technique Partner rotation (no need to bring a partner change partners) A relaxed but focused atmosphere Practical material you can use immediately on the social dance floor Our approach prioritises quality over quantity, helping you build a solid technical base while staying true to the social, playful spirit of Cuban dance. Whether you’re just starting out or looking to sharpen your skills, these classes offer a consistent space to learn, practice, and grow.

sessions.title: Rueda Libre Wednesday
sessions.details: Coal Drops Yard, Coal Drops Yard, King's Cross, London, N1C 4DQ, United Kingdom • Join Us for an Unforgettable Outdoor Cuban Salsa Event! Get ready to dance, learn, meet amazing people, and embrace the joy of salsa dancing! Our outdoor salsa event is the perfect place to spice up your evening, whether you’re a seasoned pro or have never danced a step in your life. Here’s what to expect: Cuban Salsa and Rueda Classes: We’re offering classes for all levels, from absolute beginners to advanced dancers. No partner? No problem! Everyone is welcome to join in the fun. Visit https://ruedalibre.co.uk/classes/ to find out which class level suits you best. Class Time: Classes kick off at 7:00 PM, but make sure to arrive by 6:45 PM to show your QR code and collect your wristband. This ensures you’re ready and wont miss the beginning of the class. Free Outdoor Social Dancing: After the class, the night doesn’t end! Stick around for some free outdoor social dancing. Practice your moves, connect with the community, and just let loose. The vibe is 10/10 so enjoy it. Affordable Pricing: Classes start at just £9 (excluding fees), and you’ll have the chance to practice what you’ve learned during the session. The most budget-friendly way to pay is via our QR code in person, no fees to worry about. Prefer other options? We also accept cash, contactless payments, and online payments for your convenience. Whether you’re a salsa enthusiast or simply looking for a new experience, this is your chance to dance, laugh, and create unforgettable memories. Join us and make the most of your Wednesday evening. Ready to dance? Grab your spot and let’s make this a night to remember. See you there!

sessions.title: Wednesday Moving - Juan Sanchez Plaza
sessions.details: Round Chapel Old School Rooms, Powerscroft Road, Hackney E5 0PU • Juan is the founder of The 5 Elements Practice, a holistic platform where movement, sound and meditation meet. This is an invitation into a transformative journey; to move, to sense, and to reconnect with yourself beyond the thinking mind. We begin with a guided meditation, from which movement unfolds. As always, the session is open to everyone and the space is held as an inclusive, welcoming and queer-friend environment where all bodies and identities are respected.

sessions.title: Wednesday Moving - Olly Otley
sessions.details: Round Chapel Old School Rooms, Powerscroft Road, Hackney E5 0PU • As a community dance artist and forest school leader, Olly brings a natural playfulness and a love of nature to her work. Olly moves because it makes her feel good and her mission is to share that feeling with others. She teaches a mix of guided improvisation, partner work and simple skill-building sequences.

sessions.title: Wednesday Moving - Galit Criden
sessions.details: Round Chapel Old School Rooms, Powerscroft Road, Hackney E5 0PU • Galit's RESET movement method blends elements of somatic practices, contemporary dance, disco, and free improvisation. It's designed to take you on a transformative journey where you tap into your own power, explore movement and experience a sense of liberation.

sessions.title: Wednesday Moving - Julia Testas
sessions.details: Round Chapel Old School Rooms, Powerscroft Road, Hackney E5 0PU • Julia's improvisation sessions are inspired by a combination of her own somatic research, 'dissolving', and David Zambrano's concept of 'passing through', a philosophy that suggests that movement neeeds no leader in creation. Connecting, sharing and collaborating, Julia invites you to tap into your body's intelligence and enhance your self-awareness towards cultivating greater confidence and freedom in your movement.

sessions.title: Wednesday Moving - Marianna Sfyridi
sessions.details: Round Chapel Old School Rooms, Powerscroft Road, Hackney E5 0PU • Marianna is a dancer, performer, movement specialist and pharmacist. The development of her 'Circadian Bodies' embodiment practice is rooted in her interdisciplinery background and her research into cultural narratives. The practice prepares each different body for the day ahead, weaving together principles of capoeira, dance and somatics and fostering a deep connection with bodily sensations and movement through plaful integration of mind and body.

sessions.title: Wednesday Moving - Cat Jones
sessions.details: Round Chapel Old School Rooms, Powerscroft Road, Hackney E5 0PU • Cat draws from her experience and research into release-based dance practices to encourage fluid-limbed movement in and out of the floor. Her classes are often guided by nature embedded imagery as an underscore for improvisation. Come as you are and find ways to be both porous and curious whilst moving. Then take this feeling out and beyond the studio into your week.

sessions.title: Ballet - Classical Ballet - Dmitri Gruzdev - GEN - £13
sessions.details: Instructor: Inga George (substitute) | Level: General | Room: Studio 2 | Location: Danceworks | Classes are bookable 7 days in advance. Concessions and packages are available.Please note if you have to cancel your booking for any reason, our cancellation policy is 24 hours for open classes , 48 hours for masterclasses . For our terms and conditions click here.If you have any questions or technical issues call 020 7629 6183 or email membership@danceworks.com.CLASS DESCRIPTIONThe classes are geared to help dancers maintain and further their technique, strengthen the core centre, work on their musicality and speed while at the same time investigate and explore deeper level of their artistry. The class is based on the Russian ballet style, teaching dance, musicality, and technique in a fun and engaging manner.This class is not suited for absolute beginners.Minimum age: 14 (Younger with exception, contact reception )

sessions.title: Ballet - Classical Ballet - Inga George - INT - £12
sessions.details: Instructor: Inga George | Level: Intermediate | Room: Studio 2 | Location: Danceworks | Classes are bookable 7 days in advance. Concessions and packages are available.Please note if you have to cancel your booking for any reason, our cancellation policy is 24 hours for open classes , 48 hours for masterclasses . For our terms and conditions click here.If you have any questions or technical issues call 020 7629 6183 or email membership@danceworks.com.CLASS DESCRIPTION A moving class created for students with previous ballet experience and familiarity with ballet terminology. Progress through barre and center work designed to increase strength, flexibility, and technique and dance through graceful combinations. Special attention to epaulement (turning of the shoulders and upper body), correct training of the arms (port de bras), fluidity, placement, coordination with the head and all movements and precise coordination of the whole body, working as one unit.Recommended for those with around one year’s worth of knowledge of basic ballet steps or for returning students looking to go back to the basics. Minimum age: 16 (12+ as long as they have prior experience)

For our terms and conditions click here.If you have any questions or technical issues call 020 7629 6183 or email membership@danceworks.com.CLASS DESCRIPTION In this class, there will be a flow of manageable but challenging exercises with a fun approach, designed to improve coordination, strength, flexibility, style, and musicality. Minimum age: 10

sessions.title: Ballet - Classical Ballet - Denzil Bailey - INT/ADV - £12
sessions.details: Instructor: Owen Morris (substitute) | Level: Interm/Advanced | Room: Studio 2 | Location: Danceworks | Classes are bookable 7 days in advance. Concessions and packages are available.Please note if you have to cancel your booking for any reason, our cancellation policy is 24 hours for open classes , 48 hours for masterclasses . For our terms and conditions click here.If you have any questions or technical issues call 020 7629 6183 or email membership@danceworks.com. CLASS DESCRIPTIONThese classes are for intermediate and professional level dancers, offering a versatile and engaging approach to ballet. CLASS DESCRIPTION Ballet is the perfect discipline to develop posture, alignment, strength, balance, flexibility, coordination and grace.If you are new to Ballet then my Ballet Classes for Beginners are the perfect way to start your journey. It is never too late to learn and these ballet lessons will also be suitable for those of you seeking to refresh your skills. I am a highly qualified ballet teacher and ex- professional senior Soloist and Principal with Birmingham Royal Ballet. I am also qualified to teach pilates. You are in safe hands! Topics covered will be tailored to the needs of the group. However, they will generally include: Establishment of basic ballet vocabulary and the purpose of each exercise Development of strength, flexibility and secure posture Improvement of coordination Awareness of the body in space Putting techniques into practice with simple and fun choreographic enchaînements Musicality instruction You will be given friendly feedback and support throughout the lessons. Beginner lessons provide a fun introduction to Ballet. They will cover basic ballet principles in a supportive environment. Ballet terminology will be explained and demystified. The classes will cover barre work, centre practice, allegro (jumps), pirouettes (turns) and some choreographed, exploratory enchaînements. Minimum age: 16 (14+ as long as they have prior experience)

CLASS DESCRIPTION Always wanted to try ballet but didn’t know where to start? Or maybe you danced as a child and want to return to the basics? This class is designed just for you. Absolute Beginners Ballet is a slow-paced, supportive introduction to classical ballet. You’ll learn the essential positions, steps, and techniques, all while developing strength, flexibility, posture, and coordination. No experience is needed—just a willingness to learn and move. Each session includes foundational barre work and simple centre exercises to help you feel confident and graceful, even as a beginner. We keep things light and enjoyable, blending technique with fun, flowing movement. Whether you’re curious about ballet or simply looking for a new way to move your body, this class offers a welcoming space to begin. You’ll improve your posture, balance, coordination and overall strength and flexibility.” Minimum age: 16

sessions.details: Instructor: Christina Mittelmaier | Level: All Levels | Room: Studio 3 | Location: Danceworks | Classes are bookable 7 days in advance. CLASS DESCRIPTION: Do you want to limit your injury risk, while also building strength to become a better dancer? This fitness class will help you substantially improve your posture and correct any issues relating to your body alignment. It is very easy to create bad habits in ballet, so changing these habits often requires retraining muscle memory. Christina's Posture Power with PBT elements class will not only help you build up more strength and flexibility, but will also help you train the muscle memory required for automatically performing movements the right way. This class will be accompanied by music. With her experience as a coach, ballet teacher and approved PBT instructor, Christina has put together a class that will help you limit your injury risk and become a stronger and better dancer. Posture Power with PBT elements is a general level class and therefore suitable for those, who either wish to improve their ballet technique or simply improve their fitness level. It is also a perfect preparation for her 11:30 Int/Adv ballet class which takes place right after her Posture Power with PBT elements class. Progressing Ballet Technique (PBT) is an innovative programme which was developed by Marie Walton- Mahon in order to help dancers and students improve their technique. The programme includes carefully designed exercises focusing on training muscle memory and improving core strength, weight placement and alignment of the body. Minimum age: 14

sessions.title: Contemporary & Jazz - Graham Technique - Berenice Heger-Hedloy - BEG - £13
sessions.details: Instructor: Berenice Heger-Hedloy | Level: Beginner | Room: Studio 9 | Location: Danceworks | Classes are bookable 7 days in advance. For our terms and conditions click here.If you have any questions or technical issues call 020 7629 6183 or email membership@danceworks.com. CLASS DESCRIPTION This class will follow the traditional structure of the codified technique, sequencing through a set series of seated floor exercises, standing exercises and across-the-floor sequences. The Graham Technique is an immensely valuable technique to layer on top of any dance training as it emphasises the importance of the usage of the back, as well as accessing the deep core abdominal muscles.Fundamental principles in Graham Technique are the contraction and release, opposition, shift of weight and spirals. Graham dancers believe that it is important that we not simply see ourselves as objects in space when dancing, but that we expand their body, energy and focus so they are amplified enough to fill that space. The class will serve to free up the back, open up the hips and the body. On top, form the dancer to find dynamics, intricacy and articulation of the spine. Minimum age: 16

sessions.details: Instructor: Christina Mittelmaier | Level: All Levels | Room: Studio 3 | Location: Danceworks | Classes are bookable 7 days in advance. CLASS DESCRIPTION: Do you want to limit your injury risk, while also building strength to become a better dancer? This fitness class will help you substantially improve your posture and correct any issues relating to your body alignment. It is very easy to create bad habits in ballet, so changing these habits often requires retraining muscle memory. Christina's Posture Power with PBT elements class will not only help you build up more strength and flexibility, but will also help you train the muscle memory required for automatically performing movements the right way. This class will be accompanied by music. With her experience as a coach, ballet teacher and approved PBT instructor, Christina has put together a class that will help you limit your injury risk and become a stronger and better dancer. Posture Power with PBT elements is a general level class and therefore suitable for those, who either wish to improve their ballet technique or simply improve their fitness level. It is also a perfect preparation for her 11:30 Int/Adv ballet class which takes place right after her Posture Power with PBT elements class. Progressing Ballet Technique (PBT) is an innovative programme which was developed by Marie Walton- Mahon in order to help dancers and students improve their technique.
