sessions.details: Instructor: Natalie Twist | Level: All | Room: Studio 9 | Location: In Studio Classes | A commercial class with a Twist ;)

sessions.title: Hip Hop - Locking (All) £13 Mr. Ben
sessions.details: Instructor: Mr. Ben | Level: All | Room: Studio 6 | Location: In Studio Classes | Locking, or Campbellocking, is a dance created by Don Campbell in 1970, LA.It's soulful, funky, dynamic, characterful & has precise technique & musicality.

sessions.title: Hip Hop - Hip Hop & Choreo (Int) £13 John Graham
sessions.details: Instructor: John Graham | Level: Int | Room: Studio 7 | Location: In Studio Classes | This class will utilise and explore hip hop techniques but have the freedom of blending in other styles to create a choreography aimed at expressing the mood, feel and musicality of a particular song. The class is aimed at people with some experience of dance who are confident to learn at an intermediate pace.

sessions.title: Jazz/Musical Theatre - Lyrical Jazz (All) £13 Sophie McIntosh
sessions.details: Instructor: Sophie McIntosh | Level: All | Room: Studio 79 | Location: In Studio Classes | Lyrical Jazz with Sophie M. offers dancers the chance to improve their technique while enjoying the freedom and creative expression of lyrical dance. Ideal for beginners, professionals, and everyone in between, the class will consist of a focussed warm up followed by beautiful lyrical choreography in a range of styles.

sessions.title: Tap - Tap (Int/Adv) £13 Avalon
sessions.details: Instructor: Avalon Rathgeb/Old Kent Rd | Level: Int/Adv | Room: Studio 5 | Location: In Studio Classes | This class is for the Intermediate/Advanced tap dancer that has a high standard of knowledge and understanding of all fundamental steps and musical ideas. You know how to execute some mean double shuffles, tight cramp rolls, easy looking riffs, SWINGING flaps, a spicy paddle and roll, sleek pull backs and flying wings.You may have heard of names such as Steve Condo's and can identify his rudiments, the BS Chorus may be in your back pocket, and you have an understanding of musical phrasing which isn't just in 4/4 or are interested to learn.We will always continue to work on understanding tonal quality, different approaches to the floor whilst improving your technical and rhythmical approach to your vocabulary of steps which we and sometimes use in improvisation. Tap shoes are required at this level so you can get the most out of class and EVERYONE is Welcome.

sessions.title: Fitness - Zumba (All) £11 Bima
sessions.details: Instructor: Bima Padilla | Level: All | Room: Studio 12 | Location: In Studio Classes | Join me for an exhilarating hour of dance fitness celebrating music and dance styles from all over the world, including Salsa, Merengue, Dancehall, Hip Hop (and even a splash of Kpop!) to name but a few.I’m here to dispel any preconceptions that Zumba is just shimmying and hip wiggling! Whether you’re new to fitness, looking for a high impact workout or simply just want to clear your head and de-stress, there is something for everyone in this class.

sessions.title: Hip Hop - House (All) £13 Marko
sessions.details: Instructor: Marko Stamenkovic | Level: All | Room: Studio 2 | Location: In Studio Classes | In this class students will learn how to move to the beat of house music through a combination of improvisation, rhythmic sequences, and footwork. We start with a warm up where we cover the foundational steps, we will learn variations and choreography, learn about the culture of House dance and have a good time in this high energy class.

sessions.title: Street Dance/Commercial - 80s/90s Dance! (Beg) £11 Anthony King
sessions.details: Instructor: Anthony King | Level: Beg | Room: Studio 10 | Location: In Studio Classes | The 80s|90s Dance Class! Now you can dance in the style of the 80's & 90's to the hits of Britney, Prince, Madonna, Duran Duran and more in a fun dance class which is accessible to all! Anthony King will teach you to dance to the hits of the 80's & 90's with all the iconic moves and choreography combining, commercial, street, hip-hop and various dance styles, made simple for people of all levels... with a new song and choreography each week!

sessions.title: Ballet - Improvers Ballet £12 Hannah
sessions.details: Instructor: Hannah Frost | Level: Beg | Room: Studio 11 | Location: In Studio Classes | Are you a beginner who wants to move on from basic classes but feels lost in elementary? Improvers is the class for you!This class is for people who have a basic ballet technique already and wish to progress safely to the next level. It aims to bridge the gap between beginner and elementary levels with longer, more complicated exercises and by introducing new pirouettes and jumps. It is also suitable for more advanced dancers who have had a long break or who are recovering from injury.

sessions.title: Street Dance/Commercial - Commercial Edge (All) £12 Christina Andrea
sessions.details: Instructor: Christina Andrea | Level: All | Room: Studio 1 | Location: In Studio Classes | With over 15 years in the dance industry as a choreographer, teacher and dancer, Christina’s class is aimed to help all levels improve their dance skills, have fun and learn the freshest commercial styles in an open and inclusive space.

sessions.title: Street Dance/Commercial - Sexy RnB (All 16+) £12 Natalie
sessions.details: Instructor: Natalie Twist | Level: All | Room: Studio 9 | Location: In Studio Classes | Step into a space where a fierce, “dance like no one’s watching” attitude is not just welcomed – it’s celebrated! Sexy RnB is a dance class that will leave you feeling confident, empowered, and unapologetically fabulous with iconic songs and good vibes!

sessions.title: Jazz/Musical Theatre - Musical Theatre Dance (Int/Adv) £13 George Beet
sessions.details: Instructor: George Beet | Level: All | Room: Studio 7 | Location: In Studio Classes | Each class entails a full body warm up and a routine that will work on style, performance and technique. This is a supportive space where we aim to leave inhibitions at the door and walk away feeling great and inspired from one another. Heels/ show shoes are encouraged but not necessary. Note: filming is optional for training purposes and George accommodates both filmed and non filmed groups at the end of class. You do not have to dance with any cameras out. Let's dance! Check out @BeatswithBeet on Instagram for an insight into George's work.

sessions.title: Hip Hop - Hip Hop (All) £15 Kieran Lai
sessions.details: Instructor: Substitute Teacher (substitute) | Level: All | Room: Studio 79 | Location: In Studio Classes | Open-level street choreography built on hip hop foundations. Popping, krump, and other street styles collide with creativity and musicality. Learn combos and street knowledge that hit in the cypher and on camera—while building real confidence, power, and presence on the dance floor.Kieran was a Red Bull Dance Style Champion in 2020, an Original Cast Member of & Juliet Musical 2019 - 2022, Britain's Got Talent 2014 Solo Semi-Finalist, BBC Young Dancer 2015 Wild Card Finalist, and has travelled with Next Gen Workshops to teach over 10,000 students in India. He has had commercial success working with brands like Pokemon, Samsung, Just Dance and Levis.

sessions.title: Street Dance/Commercial - Popping Basics (Beg) £13 Jutsu
sessions.details: Instructor: Jonathan Reid | Level: Beg | Room: Studio 5 | Location: In Studio Classes | Popping is one of the most amazing disciplines within the street dance world. Michael Jackson, Usher, Janet Jackson, Chris Brown, Ciara, Missy Elliot and Beyoncé are just a few names who have used this art form.No matter what your style, flavour or dance background, having popping within your vocabulary will take your dance/artistry to the next level and have you stand out from the rest.This class is a beginner/foundation class.What will it cover?• Popping foundation techniques and basics.• Basic in other styles used in popping (Waving, Tutting, Animation, Robot, Puppet Style, Gliding (Moonwalk), Mime, Boogaloo)• Control and high-level body isolation.• Tricks and blow up moves for battle, shows, auditions or just to show off on a night out.• Musicality (the different ways you can approach, play and connect with music)• Basic choreography, freestyle and how to get funky

sessions.title: Street Dance/Commercial - Soul & Party Dance (All) £11 Cathy
sessions.details: Instructor: Maau Cathy Tse | Level: All | Room: Studio 2 | Location: In Studio Classes | Soul and party dances, originally known as party dances, were widespread throughout the 1960s and early 1970s. These dances are simple, groovy, and full of enthusiasm, helping to improve your rhythm, timing, and body movements.

sessions.title: Contemporary - Contemporary (All) £13 Julia
sessions.details: Instructor: Julia Boguslavets | Level: Gen | Room: Studio 12 | Location: In Studio Classes | A mixture of contemporary, modern and jazz dance styles this class is aimed to change your idea about contemporary. Open for all levels, each session includes an intense full body warm-up and choreography with the main focus on lines building, musicality and technicality.

sessions.title: Street Dance/Commercial - House Choreo (Int) £12 Vi
sessions.details: Instructor: Vi Hiroki | Level: Int | Room: Studio 10 | Location: In Studio Classes | The class will cover・Warm-up for 10mins with some House foundations and their variation.・Technique of footwork, body control and floor work sometimes.・Fun & Challenging choreography with different type of music. (For four×eight counts)

sessions.title: Street Dance/Commercial - Commercial (16+,All) £13 Poppy Radford
sessions.details: Instructor: Poppy Radford | Level: All | Room: Studio 6 | Location: In Studio Classes | Come and join a fun, high-energy class where we train hard, dance full-out, and lift each other up. Each week we’ll focus on pick-up, performance, and musicality while learning fierce, fun, full out commercial choreography that challenges you and builds your confidence.Whether you’re new to class or a regular, this is your space to grow, push yourself, and enjoy the process. Expect a supportive vibe, big energy, and a chance to really perform while having a great time doing what you love. We work hard, dance harder — and always leave feeling better than we came in.

sessions.title: World - Reggaeton (Beg 16+) £12 Marina
sessions.details: Instructor: Marina Angemi | Level: Beg | Room: Studio 1 | Location: In Studio Classes | Come dance, sweat and feel amazing to Reggaeton - a high-energy, spicy, free-spirited Latin style!If you’re looking for a different experience in a safe, supportive space and want to have fun whilst learning, this class is for you!

sessions.title: Beginners Commercial
sessions.details: In this commercial dance class, you'll start with an energising warm-up that includes key commercial dance drills to get your body moving and ready for the session. Afterward, we’ll dive into learning a fun and dynamic routine set to a popular, upbeat song. You'll have the opportunity to develop your style, timing, and performance skills as you work through the choreography. This class is designed to help you grow and have fun while learning the latest moves! | Maisie Randle | 16 yrs and over | Studio 4

sessions.title: Improvers Commercial
sessions.details: Ready to take your dancing to the next level? Our Improvers Commercial class is perfect for those looking for a challenge! Expect high-energy combos, industry-style movement, and a focus on musicality, performance, and execution. This class will push your technique, build confidence, and help you bring that star quality to your dancing. If you're comfortable picking up choreography and ready to level up, this one’s for you! | Maisie Randle | 16 yrs and over | Studio 3

sessions.title: Beginners Heels
sessions.details: Step into confidence and unleash your inner diva with our Beginners Heels Class! Perfect for those new to dancing in heels, this class focuses on foundational skills, posture, and poise.Our experienced instructor will guide you through fun and empowering routines, teaching essential techniques from basic walks to sultry combinations. No dance experience is needed—just bring your favorite heels and get ready to shine!Join us to boost your self-confidence, improve your dance skills, and have a blast. See you on the dance floor! | Mslawrensky . | 18 yrs and over | Studio 4

sessions.title: Improvers Heels
sessions.details: Improvers Heels focuses on refining technique, enhancing fluidity, and mastering more intricate choreography. This class is designed to elevate your skills, boost confidence, and challenge you to embody strength, grace, and empowerment—all while embracing your unique, fabulous self! | Tarek Khwiss | 18 yrs and over | Studio 4

sessions.title: Beginners Old Way Vogue
sessions.details: Voguing is a dance-based art form that is just one part of Ballroom, a culture that grew from the Black and Latinx LGBTQ+ communities in prisons as well as the Harlem Drag Ball Scene. The class will not only give you an introduction to the performance style of Old Way, but a step into to wider Ballroom culture and history. | Gadget Louboutin | 16 yrs and over | Studio 3

sessions.title: Beginners Commercial
sessions.details: Learn the basics of Commercial dance in a fun and supportive environment. This class is perfect for mastering dynamic moves and building confidence on the dancefloor! | Tarek Khwiss | 16 yrs and over | Studio 4

sessions.title: Beginners Commercial
sessions.details: Discover your natural groove, lines and shapes whilst learning full out choreography to your favourite music. Each week we will explore different styles within commercial keeping it exciting and giving you all the dynamics. Come vibe with me !! | Melly Cardell | 16 yrs and over | Studio 3

sessions.title: Commercial RnB Vibes
sessions.details: Welcome into the world of Commercial RnB Vibes, where rhythm and dance meet in perfect harmony. Every beat tells a story. Join us for energetic classes that blend passion, rhythm, and fun into every step. Let's dance our way to joy and fitness, connect, and groove together at R&B Vibes | Beccy Boom | 16 yrs and over | Studio 5

sessions.title: Improvers Heels
sessions.details: Step into the world of heels dance with our Beg/Int Heels class! Designed for those looking to build a strong foundation while refining their technique, this class focuses on confidence, fluidity, and performance quality. Whether you're new to dancing in heels or looking to elevate your skills, you'll explore movement, musicality, and personal expression in a supportive and empowering environment. | Cover - Beccy Boom | 16 yrs and over | Studio 5

sessions.title: Beginners Contemporary
sessions.details: Step into the flow with me in this Beginners Contemporary Class – Together, we'll unravel the basics, polish/teach techniques, and seamlessly flow into captivating routines. It's not just a class; it's a community of dance enthusiasts. Expect a laid-back, fun atmosphere that celebrates individual expression. | George Butler | 16 yrs and over | Studio 6

sessions.title: Beginners Heels
sessions.details: Step into the world of heels dance with our Beginners Heels class! Perfect for anyone eager to explore the art of dancing in heels, this class focuses on boosting your confidence, enhancing your technique, and elevating your performance. Whether you're looking to sharpen your skills, express your unique style, or simply have fun, this class offers the perfect introduction to the captivating and empowering world of heels dance. | Cover - Beccy Boom | 18 yrs and over | Studio 2

sessions.title: Beginners Latin
sessions.details: Bring those Strictly vibes to the dance floor! This class is the perfect mix of spicy Latin rhythms and street dance attitude. We’ve stripped away the formality to create a high-energy fusion that’s all about having fun and moving to the beat.You’ll learn simple Latin-inspired moves of Cha-cha-cha, Samba, Jive, and Rumba mixed with commercial styles in a relaxed, solo-dance environment - so no partner is ever needed.Whether you're a total beginner or want to come back to dance, come along and find your rhythm. Just grab your trainers, socks, or Latin shoes if you fancy, and get ready to dance! | Anastasiia Artiushok | 16 yrs and over | Studio 4

sessions.title: Improvers Heels
sessions.details: A confidence-boosting improvers heels class focused on movement quality, clean technique, sensuality and foundational heel skills. Learn to move with quality, joy and intention. All in a supportive space, with good music, that empowers you to own your performance. | Kamilla Halid | 18 yrs and over | Studio 2

sessions.title: Improvers Contemporary
sessions.details: Step deeper into your movement in this Improvers Contemporary class. We’ll build on foundational technique, refine control and musicality, and explore more detailed choreography that challenges and inspires. Expect fluid transitions, expressive movement, and space to develop your own style. The atmosphere stays relaxed and supportive, with a strong sense of community and a focus on growing together through dance. | George Butler | 16 yrs and over | Studio 2

sessions.title: Beginners Hip Hop
sessions.details: In this Hip Hop class, we’ll break down the essential foundations and grooves that make up the heart of hip hop dance. You’ll learn key movements like isolations, body rolls, and basic footwork, all while developing your own style and flow. These foundational moves will then come together in a fun, short routine, so you can feel the rhythm and confidence to perform with ease. No experience? No problem! We’ll take it slow, step-by-step, so everyone can follow along and have a great time. By the end of class, you’ll be feeling like a pro, ready to show off your new moves! | Maisie Randle | 16 yrs and over | Studio 2

sessions.title: TRAPLORD: RELEASE THE TRAP - A Creative Wellbeing Workshop for Black Men
sessions.details: This workshop provides an intimate space for Black men to move, release, and be witnessed.To mark a decade of TRAPLORD, Ivan Michael Blackstock invites participants into a workshop that blends dance, street culture and conversation, offering a rare chance to explore vulnerability, identity, and emotional wellbeing through embodied practice and shared experience. Rooted in the creative world of the Olivier Award-winning production TRAPLORD.What to ExpectBreath work & Intention SettingArrive, ground, and call in your purpose for the session.Mind–Body Movement Session + CypherA guided movement practice followed by an open freestyle circle for expression, release, and shared energy.Q&A with Ivan Michael Blackstock + TRAPLORD FamilyA candid space for dialogue, reflection, and creative exchange.Exclusive Behind-the-Scenes Screening of TRAPLORDA private look at the creative process, unseen footage, and the evolution of the TRAPLORD world.A Gift of Flowers for the MandemBecause we believe in giving each other our flowers while we’re here — in body, spirit, and community.Who This Is For:This space is created specifically for Black men seeking a moment to reconnect with their bodies, their breath, and each other. Whether you are a dancer, a rapper, a creative or simply someone in need of release.No prior dance experience is required. Just come as you are.

sessions.title: Pre-Improvers
sessions.details:
This class focuses on building technical strength and confidence while polishing arm, head & leg coordination in preparation for Improvers 1. Studio classes will start with 10-15 mins of foot mobilising and strengthening exercises and body conditioning, particluarly good for those en pointe or training towards it. Suitable for those who are confident and polished with all Beginners 3 material (including Beginner-General) or for those already attending Improvers 1 who need a little extra help. 
There are supporting handouts and videos for each lesson.A 30 min add-on Pointe class for Complete Beginners follows the 90 minute class. • Place: New City Fitness (Studio A), Redvers Street, London, N1 6NE • Dates: 13th April - 6th July 2026(11 lessons; no class 4th & 25th May) • Cost: £185 / £250 • Teacher: Serena Cundari

sessions.title: Improvers 1
sessions.details:
Improvers 1 is for adults who are confident and polished with all the Beginners material up to and including Pre-Improvers and are ready to build on pirouettes, work on more challenging adage in the centre and start batterie. Some will benefit from attending Pre-Improvers alongside or instead, if not quite strong or polished enough yet.  There are supporting handouts and videos for each lesson. 

 You should already have attended the Complete Beginners Pointe course after Pre-Improvers on Mondays and/or be confident with relevé in retiré sideways at the barre and echappé sin the centre, before booking the Beginners Pointe class that follows. Those assessed as ready for Pointework should attend the Complete Beginners Pointe add-on with Pre-Improvers on Mondays first. • Place: Capital City College (Holloway), 444 Camden Road, London, N7 0SP • Dates: 15th April - 1st July 2026(11 lessons; no class 18 Feb) • Cost: £180 / £245 • Teacher: Serena Cundari

sessions.title: Improvers 2
sessions.details: This class is for adults who have successfully mastered all the elements in Improvers 1 and are competent with double pirouettes and ready to learn fouettés, more complex batterie such as brisé and entrechat trois and more challenging grand allegro such as grand jeté en tournant, grand fouetté sauté and saut de basque. You should attend at least twice a week to be strong enough for this level; can also be taken with Improvers 1 if less advanced or with Advanced 1 if more experienced. Those attending pointework afterwards must already be sufficiently skilled and strong enough for relevés on one leg in the centre. This course is also suitable for ballet-fit returning dancers who received vocational training to a high level. A special discount package is available on Tuesdays which starts at 6.30pm with Ballet Floor-barre, followed by Improvers 2 Class & Elementary Pointe which comes with a saving of £35. sessions.title: Tuesdays in Islington
sessions.details: Serena's Ballet Floor-Barre (BFB) consists of exercises based on Ballet steps performed lying and sitting on the floor. It's an ideal way to prepare and maintain the body for Ballet - strengthening, lengthening and toning, whilst increasing your turnout, flexibility and elasticity. It is a great complement to Ballet training. Some PBT (Progressing Ballet Technique) exercises using the small pilates ball (supplied) are included. • Place: Capital City College (Holloway), 444 Camden Road, London, N7 0SP14th April - 30th June 2026(11 lessons; no class 26 May) • Cost: £150 • Teacher: Serena CundariImprovers 2 follows at 7.30pm Save £35 when taken as part of the Tuesday Improvers 2 Discount Package

sessions.title: Absolute Beginner Ballet
sessions.details: Mondays 6:30-7:30pm w/ Iole | This class is geared towards those who have either never taken ballet or are returning from a long hiatus. You will be (re)introduced to classical terminology and movement through a classical ballet barre giving you the opportunity to build core strength, mental and physical flexibility, musicality, and leg tone. Class will conclude with centre practice, which will offer you more freedom of movement and personal creativity. All in a warm, supportive, and welcoming environment.

sessions.title: Ballet Improver
sessions.details: Mondays 7:30-9:00pm w/ Iole | Ballet Improvers offers an open and relaxed atmosphere where dancers are encouraged to take part in discussion and reflection while deepening the foundations of ballet technique. Classes progress gradually, introducing new challenges and technical layers based on the group’s rhythm and development. Iole brings a personal touch to the ballet experience, focusing on expressiveness, musicality, coordination, and the connection between body and space. Great care is given to each dancer’s individual abilities and unique way of expressing the technique.

sessions.title: Adv/Pro Contemporary
sessions.details: Tuesdays 10:00-11:30am w/ Nadine | For professional and advanced level dancers, this contemporary class will have a released based focus with elements of Cunningham technique and inspiration taken from the commercial dance world.Class will follow a similar structure each week, starting with a Gyrokinesis inspired warm up, before moving through a variety of technical exercises to address the whole body before ending with a phrase. Aiming to prepare the body and mind for the day ahead.

sessions.title: Beginner Horton
sessions.details: Tuesdays 6:00-7:30pm w/ Melissa | Discover Horton Technique in this beginner-friendly class that builds dance from the ground up. Created by Lester Horton in the 1930s/40s, the technique develops alignment, strength, flexibility, and coordination—skills that support all styles of dance. We’ll focus on core movements like flat backs, laterals, and T positions, then progress to Horton’s signature fortifications and studies. With repetition, clear guidance, and supportive pacing, you’ll gain confidence, strength, and artistry.

sessions.title: Beginner Broadway Jazz
sessions.details: Tuesdays 7:30-8:30pm w/ Isla | A high-energy jazz class with a theatrical edge. We'll start with a warm-up, then dive into choreography-blending classic jazz foundations with a performance-driven style. Expect strong musicality, big movement, and space to bring your own personality to the dance. Beginner-friendly and open to anyone who wants to move, have fun, and try something new.

sessions.title: 'Bodies in Action' - Ballet for Contemporary Dancers
sessions.details: Wednesdays and Fridays 10:00-11:30am | This class is focused on creating an inclusive and positive space for professional contemporary dancers. Everybody is welcome to engage in our ballet for contemporary dancers company class without regard to gender identity, race, body type or disability; diversity is something we pride ourselves on. We aim to provide a class that will allow the body to become more aware of its physical activity and how we can further develop and hone in on our skills as contemporary dancers.

sessions.title: Contemporary Improver
sessions.details: Wednesdays 6:30-7:30pm w/ Iole | This class invites you to move, connect, and awaken rhythm through contemporary dance blending technique and exploration. Rooted in Cunningham, release, and floorwork, it weaves dynamic crossings, phrases, and guided improvisations shaped by Iole’s distinctive touch. You’ll play with momentum, texture, and flow, expanding coordination and expression. Open to movers with some experience, each session nurtures rhythm, body awareness, and freedom through a living dialogue of technique and artistry.

sessions.title: Beginner Contemporary
sessions.details: Wednesdays 7:30-8:30pm w/ Iole | This class invites you to explore movement and its connections, focusing on the principles and introduction to contemporary dance. Through a blend of exercises, crossings, and actions, we will explore the concepts of flow and momentum. This is an opportunity to build strength and rediscover the body's potential through movement. Classes will cover the fundamental concepts of the main modern and contemporary dance techniques, including release, floor work, Cunningham, and more.

sessions.title: General Level Ballet
sessions.details: Thursdays 7:00-8:30pm w/ Isla | This open-level class welcomes dancers of all backgrounds, whether you’re returning to ballet, building confidence at the barre, or looking to refine existing skills. Isla Roebig creates a supportive and encouraging environment, balancing strong technical foundations with musicality and flow.

sessions.title: Open Level Contemporary
sessions.details: Fridays 12:00-1:15pm w/ Chloe | Join Chloe Bowman for an energising and explorative contemporary class open to all levels. Grounded in release-based technique and guided improvisation, this class invites dancers to connect breath, weight, and flow through dynamic movement phrases. Chloe’s teaching emphasises musicality, individuality, and presence — creating a space where beginners feel supported and experienced movers can deepen their practice. Expect a thoughtful balance of structure and freedom, culminating in expansive, expressive choreography that celebrates movement in the moment.

sessions.title: Ballet Improver
sessions.details: Saturdays 12:45-1:45pm w/ Iole | Ballet Improvers offers an open and relaxed atmosphere where dancers are encouraged to take part in discussion and reflection while deepening the foundations of ballet technique. Classes progress gradually, introducing new challenges and technical layers based on the group’s rhythm and development. Iole brings a personal touch to the ballet experience, focusing on expressiveness, musicality, coordination, and the connection between body and space. Great care is given to each dancer’s individual abilities and unique way of expressing the technique.

sessions.title: Beginner Pointe (+Ballet Improver class prior)
sessions.details: Saturdays 2:00-2:30pm w/ Iole | Beginner Pointe is offered as a gentle extension of Ballet Improvers, designed for dancers who are ready to begin exploring pointe work in a supportive and attentive environment. This class introduces the fundamental principles of dancing en pointe at a gradual and thoughtful pace. Dancers will focus on correct alignment, strength, balance, and articulation of the feet, while deepening their understanding of placement and coordination developed in the improvers class. ​ Following the same open and reflective atmosphere, dancers are encouraged to listen closely to their bodies and progress with care and patience. Iole offers individual guidance to help each dancer build confidence, stability, and ease on pointe, while nurturing musicality and expressiveness. The emphasis is on developing strength safely, refining technique, and discovering the unique experience of dancing en pointe as a natural continuation of each dancer’s ballet journey.

sessions.title: Skull Rub & Clay Can Dance: Clay Sessions @ Aaja, Deptford
sessions.details: Join us for the first in a series of guided afternoon hand building sessions. Think ambient & soulful tunes, meditation & clay sculptures, along with some laughs. Materials provided*. Music curated by Skullrub. 1230 - 2.30 pm clay sessions. We'll start off with a small meditation and some warm up exercises, followed by a longer pinch pot and coiling session. There will be an optional 20 minute coffee break. 1430 til late afternoon: Unguided clay session continues along with the tunes. Hang around get a drink and enjoy the music. * Air dry clay will be provided.

sessions.title: Jules Cunningham Dance Workshop
sessions.details: Jules Cunningham (they/them), originally from Liverpool, worked as an award-winning dancer for 20+ years with Merce Cunningham Dance Company and Michael Clark Company. Jules founded Julie Cunningham & Company in 2017, to create and present work that combines clarity of form with an interest in gender identity and the body. Jules uses movement language that draws on their technical dance training, expanding and queering it collaboratively, working between sound, text, visual art. Creative work is informed by solo and collaborative movement exploration, queerness, lived experience of mental illness, disability and exclusion based on Jules’ working-class background and non-binary identity. This workshop is part of a series curated by the artist Mica Georgis as part of a Residency at The Writers’ Room, Florence Trust in April 2026. The workshops are open to dancers and non-dancers. No previous dance experience is necessary.

sessions.title: 5Rhythms with Flow
sessions.details: Colet House, 151 Talgarth Rd, London W14 9DA • In 5Rhythms, we hand over prayer to the body, allowing it to become our wisest teacher. Through movement, we invite the body to express its unique rhythm in the present moment, uncovering its often suppressed intelligence.Flow Vulk brings her own energy and vision to the dance. In her words:"I found my feet on a 5Rhythms dance floor in 2014. This is when and where it all magnificently fell apart before falling into place and making sense. It is in our bones, blood and breath - the beat, the rhythm, the Song of Songs. The Song of Life. Dance it. This is how we wake up, how we become alive again, how we find answers and resolutions. We dance not to forget but to remember, not to get lost but to find ourselves, not to disappear but to arise in our full, authentic glory. So let this be an open invitation to shake off the cobwebs, rattle those bones, let that breath flow and let your feet find their beat. Let Life In."All are welcome, whether you’re new to 5Rhythms or have danced it for years.In 5Rhythms, we hand over prayer to the body, allowing it to become our wisest teacher. Through movement, we invite the body to express its unique rhythm in the present moment, uncovering its often suppressed intelligence.Flow Vulk brings her own energy and vision to the dance. In her words:"I found my feet on a 5Rhythms dance floor in 2014. This is when and where it all magnificently fell apart before falling into place and making sense. It is in our bones, blood and breath - the beat, the rhythm, the Song of Songs. The Song of Life. Dance it. This is how we wake up, how we become alive again, how we find answers and resolutions. We dance not to forget but to remember, not to get lost but to find ourselves, not to disappear but to arise in our full, authentic glory. So let this be an open invitation to shake off the cobwebs, rattle those bones, let that breath flow and let your feet find their beat. Let Life In."All are welcome, whether you’re new to 5Rhythms or have danced it for years.

sessions.title: The Movements
sessions.details: Colet House, 151 Talgarth Rd, London W14 9DA • Meditation through movementThe Movements are a collection of sacred dances, exercises, prayers and their accompanying music that originated from various schools, dervish orders and monasteries across the Middle East and Central Asia. They were collected and brought to the West by the philosopher and mystic, G.I. Gurdjieff. They were first taught in England to Ouspensky’s pupils at Lyne Place and Colet House in the 1930s. The Study Society is unique in teaching the early repertoire of Movements as they were originally taught, in an unchanged line to the present day.The Movements provide a non-verbal way of experiencing the essence of the Fourth Way teaching. They convey knowledge about the great universal laws and can be a vehicle for expressing universal truths. They can be thought of as a special form of ‘moving meditation’. Through the practice of attention they can enable participants to experience the working of finer energies, bringing all parts of the human organism into a more harmonious whole.The Study Society offers these evenings free of charge, but you are invited to make a donation to support the charity in its works.Meditation through movementThe Movements are a collection of sacred dances, exercises, prayers and their accompanying music that originated from various schools, dervish orders and monasteries across the Middle East and Central Asia. They were collected and brought to the West by the philosopher and mystic, G.I. Gurdjieff. They were first taught in England to Ouspensky’s pupils at Lyne Place and Colet House in the 1930s. The Study Society is unique in teaching the early repertoire of Movements as they were originally taught, in an unchanged line to the present day.The Movements provide a non-verbal way of experiencing the essence of the Fourth Way teaching. They convey knowledge about the great universal laws and can be a vehicle for expressing universal truths. They can be thought of as a special form of ‘moving meditation’. Through the practice of attention they can enable participants to experience the working of finer energies, bringing all parts of the human organism into a more harmonious whole.The Study Society offers these evenings free of charge, but you are invited to make a donation to support the charity in its works.

sessions.title: Somatic Dance: The Body as Home
sessions.details: Colet House, 151 Talgarth Rd, London W14 9DA • A Calling Home – The Body as Home is a Somatic Activated Healing™ (SAH Method) practice, a trauma-informed, body-based approach that blends: somatic movement breath work mantra and meditation music and free, intuitive movement.Rather than intellectualizing or talking about emotions, this practice invites the body to lead, allowing sensations and stored emotions to be expressed and gently released through movement. We work from a bottom-up approach, using the body and felt sensations as the entry point, rather than analyzing thoughts or revisiting specific traumatic memories. As a certified SAH Method™ teacher, I guide participants to “drop the story and be with the feeling,” creating a safe space for emotions to move without re-engaging with the narratives around them. Participants can expect a guided, supportive experience that helps to: ground and regulate the nervous system reconnect with physical sensations gently release what has been held in the body cultivate moments of safety, steadiness, and embodied presence There is no choreography, no performance, and no “right way” to move. This session is an invitation to move, shake, soften, and play, to let energy flow more freely through the body, release what feels stuck, and reconnect with joy, aliveness, and a sense of home within.Still wondering what to expect? Here is a taster of what awaits you: https://www.instagram.com/reel/DWRIaBOjPXs/?igsh=Z2luenR6amVsM3gyA Calling Home – The Body as Home is a Somatic Activated Healing™ (SAH Method) practice, a trauma-informed, body-based approach that blends: somatic movement breath work mantra and meditation music and free, intuitive movement.Rather than intellectualizing or talking about emotions, this practice invites the body to lead, allowing sensations and stored emotions to be expressed and gently released through movement. sessions.title: Playfulness for Beginners (4-week course)
sessions.details: Colet House, 151 Talgarth Rd, London W14 9DA • 7.15-9.00pm on Wednesdays June 3rd, 10th, 17th and 24thWho says your spiritual practice needs to be serious? What if playfulness could become a pathway towards self-discovery and wholeness?Come and join us for this 4-week introductory ‘practice of playfulness' journey, where grown up adults can reconnect with their often buried sense of childlike play. We will draw from the worlds of clowning, improv, mime and dance as well as spontaneous silliness and generalised irreverence. Together, this unlikely cocktail will leave you feeling joyous, open-hearted and flooded with endorphins.Through a range of games and structures, we will explore aspects of play such as spontaneity, misbehaviour, exuberance, sensitivity, connection and totality - and also take time to explore and process how we are feeling as we undergo the journey.The workshops will be a safe space with no pressure ‘to perform’- although the urge may arise. Rather the aim is to build an encouraging supportive nest from which you can launch yourself into your innately exuberant and playful aspect and free your inner child.Some aspects of the workshop will be held and structured, others will be a little bit free and spontaneous (and even occasionally anarchic), all interspersed with dancing and 'digestion time.' There will also be a chill-out area should you need to sit things out for a while ************* During this course you will: Discover how The Practice of Playfulness is a healing medicine that can transform emotions such as repression, numbness and disconnection into feelings of happiness, optimism and inner aliveness.See how the path of self-expression develops inner qualities such as courage, creativity, confidence and improved communication skills.Understand how play gives a strong taste of emotional freedom and moment-by-moment living in a real, embodied way.Be supported through regular sharing circles and times for self-reflection. ************* Read other people's experience of the course:“I believe that what Ailon is offering is incredibly powerful and if you want to live life with a bit more lightheartedness then you should definitely go along to his workshops!”“Thank you for an enriching and ridiculous 6 weeks of playfulness.I loved the journey and cultivating the trust to play together allowed fresh and unexpected expressions to emerge.”“For me this is less of a class or workshop and more of a happening…a congregation of joyful souls happy to hang out and relax in a climate of friendliness. Out of that trust, true silliness and fun can emerge.”“Thank you for bringing into fruition a space which is so playful and nourishing to the soul. To have all our parts to come up and be met, not only with with encouragement but with love is so rare in this world- it takes weird and deep to awaken and meet weird and deep!”“ It’s been a pleasure being on this journey with every single one of you and see everyone expand and grow on this journey into deeper expression of our selves. I appreciate everyone’s acceptance and kindness. It’s been the first place in a long time where I felt safe and accepted to just be myself and explore parts of myself no matter how crazy, silly and unexpected.”"Ailon creates such a safe and humorous space for people to explore their inner joker and let go of their inhibitions. I’ve given myself permission to be playful again and it’s been so liberating to reconnect with that part of me."“What a pleasure and a privilege to be part of this workshop” “Like being drunk with no alcohol”****************They can't all be wrong ???Any questions email: ailon@schoolofplayfulness.co.ukTo read more about Ailon (the founder)'s journey into playfulness have a look herehttps://ailonfreedman.substack.com/p/birthing-the-school-of-playfulness7.15-9.00pm on Wednesdays June 3rd, 10th, 17th and 24thWho says your spiritual practice needs to be serious? sessions.title: The Yoga of Vocal Sound, Music and Meditation
sessions.details: Colet House, 151 Talgarth Rd, London W14 9DA • Are you ready to embark on a journey of self-transformation? Join James D’Angelo – a leading authority on sound healing – in uncovering one of the great aspects of attaining true sound health through the tuning of the chakras.In this unique workshop, James will introduce you to vocal homeopathy, and its processes by which the chakras are stimulated and re-tuned, clearing blockages and releasing fine energies. Alongside the therapeutic use of voice, he will bring in embodiment practices with Chi Kung-like movements integrating them into the toning practices. Finally, and complementary to the chakra work, you will experience samples of Taoist healing sounds/movements. Learning mantras for the subtle energy field, and drawing vocal sound within as a form of meditation.No previous experience is necessary, only a deep intent to be your true Self.James D’Angelo has been leading his Soundspirit courses in the UK, Europe and the USA since 1996 and is considered a leading authority on sound healing practices. He is the author of The Healing Power of the Human Voice and Seed Sounds for Tuning the Chakras: Vowels, Consonants and Seed Syllables for Spiritual Transformation. www.soundspirit.co.ukAre you ready to embark on a journey of self-transformation? sessions.title: Baduanjin Qigong (8-week course)
sessions.details: Colet House, 151 Talgarth Rd, London W14 9DA • BADUANJIN QIGONG – The Eight Silk BrocadesAn Eight-Week Qigong Course with Natalia Miralles Huete📅 Course dates: 8 classes from 9 April to 4 June (No class on 23 April)18:30 – 19:30📍 Location: Top Studio, Colet HouseBaduanjin, also known as “The Eight Silk Brocades”, is an ancient Qigong set of eight different fluid movements that together condition the whole body externally — stretching and strengthening it — and internally, improving the function of the organs and systems (respiratory, circulatory, digestive, nervous, etc.).The set was first described in an eighth-century Taoist text, and it is known to have been used effectively throughout the 17th century during wars in China to alleviate soldiers’ fatigue. This is a key aspect of Qigong, as it helps us to regain energy and vitality to go about our modern daily lives and face our inner battles of stress and emotional challenges.Baduanjin represents an excellent short daily health routine. It will provide you with a whole-body workout, mobilising all your joints, strengthening your muscles and bones, and improving your posture and balance, while helping to focus and calm your busy mind. Your emotions will also feel more balanced, and your energy levels will increase.Once you have learnt the set, it will take you just 20 minutes to complete, although the practice can be shorter or longer depending on the number of repetitions. If you persevere in practising the set daily or a few times per week after the course, you will soon notice the health benefits it brings.The course also includes the essential foundations for effective Qigong practice: breathing techniques, preparatory self-massage, structured joint-opening exercises, and the “three regulations” — body, breath and mind — providing a clear and methodical framework for practising Baduanjin safely and efficiently.BADUANJIN QIGONG – The Eight Silk BrocadesAn Eight-Week Qigong Course with Natalia Miralles Huete📅 Course dates: 8 classes from 9 April to 4 June (No class on 23 April)18:30 – 19:30📍 Location: Top Studio, Colet HouseBaduanjin, also known as “The Eight Silk Brocades”, is an ancient Qigong set of eight different fluid movements that together condition the whole body externally — stretching and strengthening it — and internally, improving the function of the organs and systems (respiratory, circulatory, digestive, nervous, etc.).The set was first described in an eighth-century Taoist text, and it is known to have been used effectively throughout the 17th century during wars in China to alleviate soldiers’ fatigue. This is a key aspect of Qigong, as it helps us to regain energy and vitality to go about our modern daily lives and face our inner battles of stress and emotional challenges.Baduanjin represents an excellent short daily health routine. sessions.title: Human Garage Fascia Maneuver Workshop
sessions.details: Colet House, 151 Talgarth Rd, London W14 9DA • Fascia Maneuvers by Human Garage Workshop – March Tuesday SeriesReady to unlock stuck trauma & energy from your body? With Human Garage Fascia Maneuvers, you can master a quiet mind and rejuvenated body!Fascial Maneuver Workshop- Guided by Human Garage Certified CoachJoin us for a restorative Fascia Maneuver Workshop — a guided journey of movement, breath, and awareness designed to release stored tension and trauma within the body’s connective tissue. Combining posture, breathwork, and mindful intention, this experience helps realign the body, expand mobility, and encourage emotional balance. Discover how gentle, self-applied techniques can unlock your body’s natural flow and restore harmony from the inside out.What are Fascia Maneuvers?Fascial Maneuvers, created by Human Garage, are self-applied movements & breathwork techniques that release tension and trauma stored in the fascia—the connective tissue around muscles and organs. Combining posture, breath, intention, motion, they restore alignment, mobility, emotional balance.Why participate in this Fascia Maneuver workshop?Release built-up tension and trauma held in the body’s connective tissues, restore natural alignment, and support emotional release and flow.What to expect?Participants will practice guided self-movements, breathwork, posture shifts, and intention to gradually unlock fascial restrictions and expand mobility in a safe, supportive setting. PLEASE BRING A YOGA MAT, WATER and Curious Mind!Precautions and Limitations:Not suitable during acute injury, open wounds, deep vein thrombosis, unstable spine, or for those with conditions such as uncontrolled hypertension or severe osteoporosis. Please consult your healthcare provider if pregnant or managing serious health issues.Fascia Maneuvers by Human Garage Workshop – March Tuesday SeriesReady to unlock stuck trauma & energy from your body? sessions.title: Luminous New Moon  x The Sanctuary &Soul
Contemporary Dance StreamMovement Workshop
sessions.details: StreamMovement Dance Workshop ExperienceThis experience is all about connecting your body and mind with the body and mind of others in a kind and creative setting. Come connect with us and flow freely.In this in-person workshop we dive into the magic of StreamMovement dance techniques. Whether you're new or experienced, all are welcome to our studio where we combine improvisation, choreography, partnering and creation tasks along with physical training, breath awareness and somatic connection.

sessions.title: Expressive Movement and Dance Improvisation with musician Louis LaPorte
Women-only Vocal / Nasheeds (Beginner)
sessions.details: Laban Building • Creekside, London, SE8 3DZ, UK, London • This women-only beginner class is built around movement, rather than by a heavy beat or familiar lyrical structure. Instead of music, the class uses vocals and/or nasheeds, offering a different kind of soundscape for movement that feels stripped back, open and deeply personal. The class is ideal for women who want to step away from secular music or music altogether, and for those who are looking for a space that feels culturally sensitive, private and genuinely welcoming. It is also a beautiful option for women who want to experience dance in a different way, where the body, mind and breath have more space to lead. Movement in this class can feel soft, expressive, grounding, or whatever it needs to be for you. Because there is less sound to lean on, the class creates an eye-opening experience: you are invited to trust your own instincts, your own rhythm and your own interpretation. That can feel honest, freeing and surprisingly liberating. The atmosphere values community, uplift and comfort, so you can come solo or with a friend and feel held in the room. Who it’s for Women looking for a safe, private and culturally sensitive space to dance for the first time or return to movement after a break. Those who value community as much as the dance itself, whether they come solo or with a friend. Women who prefer to move without secular music or without music altogether. Women who want a different sound and a different relationship to movement. What to expect You will begin with a short introduction before moving into a beginner-friendly session that includes guided movement, simple choreography and space for self-expression. The class is designed to help you feel comfortable, supported and free to move at your own pace. Every ticket includes a goody bag and professional filming is available for anyone who would like. Outcome You leave feeling grounded, liberated and more connected to your own movement, with a stronger sense of confidence and self-expression. Practical note Please arrive 10–15 minutes early so you can settle in and get familiar with the space before class begins. Suitable for most fitness levels. If you are pregnant, postnatal or managing an injury, please let the team know in advance so adjustments can be made. What to Wear Anything you feel comfortable in and able to move freely. This class is 18+ only. By purchasing a ticket, you agree to these terms and conditions.

sessions.title: Women-only Music (Beginner)
sessions.details: Laban Building • Creekside, London, SE8 3DZ, UK, London • This women-only beginner class offers a joyful, energetic space for women who want to move with music and feel fully themselves while doing it. It carries the same welcoming spirit as the vocal/nasheeds class, but with a different sound and a more playful, high-energy feel. The class is designed for women who want to exist in a women-only space and move with freedom, confidence and ease. It is especially suitable for complete beginners and for those returning to movement after a break, because the atmosphere is intentionally supportive, non-judgemental, and easy to settle into. Here, “fully express yourself” means moving with honesty and personality, letting your body take up space, letting your energy show and allowing the music to carry you rather than hold you back. The class invites you to be playful, to be open and to enjoy the process without needing to get it perfect. The structure follows the same general class rhythm as the vocal/nasheeds session. Who it’s for Women looking for a safe, private, and culturally sensitive space to dance for the first time or return to movement after a break. Those who value community as much as the dance itself, whether they come solo or with a friend. Complete beginners. Women returning to dance after time away. What to expect You’ll move through a short introduction, beginner-friendly warm-up, accessible choreography and space to explore movement in a way that feels personal and fun. The focus is on confidence, enjoyment and freedom rather than perfection. Every ticket includes a goody bag and professional filming is available for anyone who would like. Outcome You leave feeling lighter, more confident, more energised and more connected to yourself. Practical note Please arrive 10–15 minutes early so you can settle in and get familiar with the space before class begins. Suitable for most fitness levels. If you are pregnant, postnatal or managing an injury, please let the team know in advance so adjustments can be made. What to Wear Anything you feel comfortable in and able to move freely. This class is 18+ only. By purchasing a ticket, you agree to these terms and conditions.

sessions.title: Mixed Intermediate / Advanced
sessions.details: Laban Building • Creekside, London, SE8 3DZ, UK, London • This mixed intermediate/advanced class is designed for dancers who already have a strong movement background and want to continue growing in both skill and artistry. It is suited to emerging dancers, pre-professionals, professionals and those with at least two years of dance experience, whether self-taught or formally trained. Contemporary dance is the foundation of the class, but the work may draw from a range of styles within that umbrella, creating a space where technique and self-expression work together. The class is not only about precision; it is also about self-actualisation, presence, and learning how to move with clarity, confidence and intention. This is a class for dancers who want to be challenged. The choreography is more complex, the technical work is more demanding, and the creative exploration asks you to think, adapt and push yourself further. Strength, flexibility, performance quality and movement vocabulary are all developed through the session. Who it’s for Dancers with prior experience who are looking to challenge themselves both technically and creatively. Emerging dancers, pre-professionals, professionals and those with at least two years of dance experience. People who want to combine self-expression with strong technique and artistic development. What to expect You will move through a short introduction, a focused warm-up, contemporary technique, complex choreography and creative work that supports both individual expression and technical growth. The class is structured to stretch you physically and creatively while still offering support and clarity. Every ticket includes a goody bag and professional filming is available for anyone who would like. Outcome You leave with stronger technique, a deeper performance presence, more confidence in your movement choices and a greater sense of creative articulation. Practical note Please arrive 10–15 minutes early so you can settle in and get familiar with the space before class begins. If you are pregnant, postnatal or managing an injury, please let the team know in advance so adjustments can be made. What to Wear Anything you feel comfortable in and able to stretch freely. This class is 18+ only. By purchasing a ticket, you agree to these terms and conditions.

sessions.title: Mixed Beginner Class
sessions.details: Laban Building • Creekside, London, SE8 3DZ, UK, London • This mixed beginner class is a welcoming, no-judgement space for anyone who is new to dance or returning after time away. It is built to feel accessible from the start, with a structure that helps you settle in quickly and build confidence as you go. One of the reasons this class works so well for beginners is that choreography begins early in the session, including from the warm-up. That means you are not left watching for too long or overthinking what to do. Instead, you are gently supported into copying, moving and feeling comfortable in the room right away. The class supports confidence, coordination, enjoyment and expression all at once. It is suitable for most fitness levels, and it is designed to help people who may not yet feel like dancers begin to trust their bodies, their memory and their ability to learn. If you have never danced before, you can expect clear guidance, repetition, encouragement and a friendly pace. You do not need to arrive knowing the steps, knowing the counts or knowing what kind of dancer you are. You simply need to come willing to try. Who it’s for Complete beginners. People returning to dance after a break. Adults looking for a welcoming, low-pressure space to move. Anyone who wants to build confidence alongside coordination and expression. What to expect You will be guided through a short introduction, accessible warm-up, simple choreography and movement that is designed to help you learn by doing. The class is supportive, structured and intentionally easy to settle into. Every ticket includes a goody bag and professional filming is available for anyone who would like. Outcome You leave feeling more confident, more comfortable in your body and more open to dance as something you can genuinely do. Practical note Please arrive 10–15 minutes early so you can settle in and get familiar with the space before class begins. Suitable for most fitness levels. If you are pregnant, postnatal or managing an injury, please let the team know in advance so adjustments can be made. What to Wear Anything you feel comfortable in and able to stretch freely. This class is 18+ only. By purchasing a ticket, you agree to these terms and conditions.

sessions.title: Women-only Stretch Class
sessions.details: Laban Building • Creekside, London, SE8 3DZ, UK, London • This women-only stretch class is a highly requested add-on for anyone who wants a low-pressure space dedicated entirely to stretching. It is not yoga. Instead, it is a guided group fitness session built to improve flexibility, mobility and range of motion through focused, full-body stretching techniques. The class combines static stretching, dynamic movement and PNF-style contract-relax work, making it both restorative and functional. It is designed for people who want to carve out time to stretch properly, without rushing and without needing to fit it into a bigger dance or fitness class. The women-only environment adds an extra layer of comfort and relatability, making it easier to switch off, settle in and focus on your own body. By the end of the session, you should feel physically lighter, taller, more open and less stiff, with a calmer mind and a stronger sense of connection to your body. Because stretching time is often overlooked in busy schedules, this class creates a rare space where you can slow down, move with care, and leave feeling both released and re-energised. Who it’s for Women who want a calm, private, and comfortable space to stretch. Women who want to improve flexibility, mobility and range of motion. Women who prefer a guided stretch session rather than yoga. Women who want time to release tension and reset. What to expect You will move through a guided stretch and mobility session that combines careful, full-body work with space to breathe, release and settle into the room. The pace is controlled, supportive and focused on making the body feel more open and available. Every ticket includes a goody bag. Outcome You leave feeling looser, freer, more open through the body and more mentally settled. Practical note Please arrive 10–15 minutes early so you can settle in and get familiar with the space before class begins. If you are pregnant, postnatal, or managing an injury, please let the team know in advance so adjustments can be made. What to Wear Anything you feel comfortable in and able to stretch freely. This class is 18+ only. By purchasing a ticket, you agree to these terms and conditions.

sessions.title: Mixed All Levels Class
sessions.details: Laban Building • Creekside, London, SE8 3DZ, UK, London • This mixed all-levels class is designed to bring together people with different dance backgrounds in one shared space. The class is less technical and places more emphasis on feeling expressive, present and connected in the room. The balance here is roughly 70% expression and 30% technique. That means there is still structure, clarity and growth, but there is also plenty of room for alternative movement and individual interpretation. The session is adaptable, so beginners and more experienced dancers can both engage meaningfully without the class feeling too difficult or too simple. This class is especially valuable for the sense of community it creates. You are not being measured only by ability level; instead, you are welcomed into a space where different types of movers can push, motivate and inspire one another. If you are unsure whether this class is for you, it is usually best to start with a beginner class and then speak to a member of the 1Syllable team, or email us, so we can help guide you to the right level. Who it’s for Adults of different dance backgrounds who want a shared class experience. People who want expression, creativity and community alongside movement. Beginners who are ready for more space and variety. More experienced dancers who want a class that feels open, adaptable and motivating. What to expect You will move through a short introduction, warm-up, guided movement and choreography that can be adapted to different experience levels. The class prioritises expression and community while still offering enough structure to help you grow. Every ticket includes a goody bag and professional filming is available for anyone who would like. Outcome You leave feeling challenged, supported, creatively open and part of a community that values expression as much as ability. Practical note Please arrive 10–15 minutes early so you can settle in and get familiar with the space before class begins. If you are pregnant, postnatal or managing an injury, please let the team know in advance so adjustments can be made. What to Wear Anything you feel comfortable in and able to stretch freely. This class is 18+ only. By purchasing a ticket, you agree to these terms and conditions.
